name: =?UTF-8?Q?Eduardo_Ver=C3=A1stegui?=
subject: Friend, your Christmas gift can be doubled — and save a child
body:
Your gift can be doubled to help bring a child home before Christmas.

| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e1093276/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvcHBycGh6YzR1UA/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsUW9MU1dlQg/qP2hpZHM9bmM1cVBTWlI5dHFlOExscw/gaUp7ng/s0s88d7c964)
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Friend,
---
Christmas is now only one week away. **One week... for a child who has not felt safe in years.**
**One week... for a child praying in the dark for someone to come.**
**One week... for us to help bring a child home before Christmas morning.** I’m writing to you again because the need has only grown more urgent. Since this might be the first time you’ve heard from me, I'd like to introduce myself and tell you why I am pleading for your help today. My name is **Eduardo Verástegui**.
I am the producer of the film _Sound of Freedom_ , and the founder of the **Sound of Freedom Foundation** , an organization created with one purpose: **To stop the sale of God’s children.**
We protect, rescue and bring them home. **This is holy work.**
And today, I want to invite you into it. Right now — even as you read this — there are boys and girls hidden in rooms they cannot leave.
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
No Christmas tree. No lights. No warm voices.
Only fear... and silence... and the terrible lie that no one is coming for them. But that lie can be broken.
And it can be broken **through you.** Because this month — during December — **every gift is being matched dollar for dollar** by a generous partner of the Sound of Freedom Foundation. **That means your compassion becomes twice as strong.**
**Your generosity becomes twice as powerful.**
**Your love pushes twice as many children toward safety and home.** **$25 Gift Doubles to $50**
**$50 Gift Doubles to $100**
**$100 Gift Doubles to $200**
**$250 Gift Doubles to $500**
**$500 Gift Doubles to $1,000** **[Now, just one week before Christmas, is your chance to help bring a child home in time for the holiday.](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)**
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ Yes, 2x My Gift To Bring A Child Home ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
**This matching opportunity ends December 31st...**
but the chance to help a child before Christmas ends in days. Scripture tells us how precious children are to God. Jesus said, _“Whoever receives one such child in My name receives Me.”_ (Matthew 18:5)

And again, _“It is not the will of your Father in heaven that one of these little ones should perish.”_ (Matthew 18:14) **These are not small verses.**
**They are the heartbeat of God.**
And in this sacred season, they are His call to each of us. When you give, you step into Jesus’ command to protect “the little ones.” You say with your actions: **Not one child will be forgotten.**
**Not one child will be for sale.**
**Not one child will be abandoned — not on my watch.** And yet time is running out. There are still children waiting tonight. Children whose names were changed to hide them.
Children who don’t know what day it is.
Children who have been told no one cares for them.
Children who fall asleep believing they are alone in the world.
---
[ Yes, 2x My Gift ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Your gift speaks a different truth —
a truth Jesus Himself spoke over every child: _“_** _Let the little children come to Me...”_******(Matthew 19:14) Your generosity brings a child closer to safety... closer to healing... closer to home. And because your gift is doubled in December, that journey becomes twice as strong. The **Sound of Freedom Foundation** works with trusted partners across the world to:
---
* Support rescue missions
* Assist with investigations
* Provide safe beds for children the moment they are freed
* Offer trauma counseling and aftercare
* Help reunite children with family whenever possible
* Advocate for laws that protect children

---
Every step depends on someone like you deciding: **“Not this Christmas. Not while I can help bring a child home.”** I want you to imagine something with me. A child wakes up in a safe bed after months — maybe years — of fear.
A soft blanket. A quiet room.
Someone kind whispering, **“You are safe now. No one will hurt you here.”** Then the sacred work begins: calling relatives... confirming documents... praying with a mother who never stopped believing... taking every step needed to bring that child home. And then — one day — the miracle happens. A door at an airport opens.
A mother looks up.
A child steps forward.
And the world stops. Tears.
A whispered, **“Thank You, God.”** **This is what your generosity can help make possible.**
**This is what it means to Bring a Child Home for Christmas.**
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ Yes, 2x My Gift ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Christmas is the story of God stepping into our darkness to bring His children home. It is the story of rescue, hope and love stronger than any evil in this world.

But traffickers do not rest during Christmas week.
They are still exploiting, selling, grooming, and destroying the lives of the innocent.

So we cannot rest either.

That is why I am asking you — urgently, humbly, personally — to **give your most generous gift today, while it can still be doubled.**

Please do not wait.
Do not set this aside.
We have only one week left to bring a child closer to home before Christmas morning.

**Your gift today can help rescue a child.**
**Your gift today can help restore a child.**
**Your gift today can help bring a child home.**

And because your gift is doubled in December, every dollar becomes two for hope... two for healing... two for freedom.****

**May God bless you for caring.**
**May He bless you for standing for His little ones.**
**And may He fill your home with peace and joy this Christmas, knowing a child may be safe because YOU chose to act.**

With all my heart, Eduardo Verástegui
Sound of Freedom, Producer
For the Sound of Freedom Foundation
---
[ ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
P.S. Your gift — doubled in December — can help welcome a child into safety, healing, and home.

Please give today.
---
[ **Support the Sound of Freedom** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
6671 Southwest Freeway, Suite 700, Houston TX 77074
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e1093276/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsUW9MU1dlQg/qP2hpZHM9bmM1cVBTWlI5dHFlOExscw/gaUp7ng/s3l23023161)

We have only one week left to bring a child closer to home before Christmas morning. Your gift today can help rescue a child. Your gift today can help restore a child. Your gift today can help bring a child home. And because your gift is doubled in December, every dollar becomes two for hope... two for healing... two for freedom. May God bless you for caring. May He bless you for standing for His little ones. And may He fill your home with peace and joy this Christmas, knowing a child may be safe because YOU chose to act. With all my heart, Eduardo Verástegui Sound of Freedom, Producer For the Sound of Freedom Foundation P.S. Your gift — doubled in December — can help welcome a child into safety, healing, and home. Please give today. 6671 Southwest Freeway, Suite 700, Houston TX 77074

# Tuesday, December 23, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1936, actor [Frederic Forrest](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO4yAURb8Gd7HgYUNcUKy0cpdtt0TYPJIngYnwmzj5-5HnF6a6xb26Rzr_b_4WKNN2_xcKOn6n6jc89ozM2DxmXLnRSvxpgXH_et4pbJyyBwnjRcEFtI-B8scPXXSLjJPq0ClrzDApraB7OGuuyqJdRznJYTJK23EKAYKJaYmQ1o7ceaYAtAKpRtVrSIvUVzA4jVZrKQaJkbi2nh_I-A57yp_6wtavtXTZPZifu9B_BMwC5uM4eipxOUsB8xYKnlGklCDtVcC8UD2nem6YvNB_t-Lryy9U_V665uLzoJxpN1YM8l4C5R8O_87Oy8F3AAAA__8zEXcd) was born in Waxahachie. He is best known for his roles in several films directed by Francis Ford Coppola, including _The Conversation_ (1974), _Apocalypse Now_ (1979), and _One from the Heart_ (1981). Recent snowy owl sightings across the Midwest and as far south as Kentucky suggest the Arctic raptors could [venture to Texas](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDvO5CAQhE9jZ1jQgB8BwSaTzaYbWti0xy3hh6B3GN9-5dHe4M8-VUlVpfrzHJ-eIu2v335Dx5_lGHcsOSIzphEjzpxoJr6SZ8x_zxf5nZc4ggQrFAjQY_AUr9HUwU0yDKpGp7q2NYPSCurVhQlB2b6XobVGLb0d-m4a5nZAKWVnTU3uDlMAWoFUVjUalknqHlocbKe1rIzEQHykhldk_Pi8xOt4Y2rmY6ujW5nPXOlfFTwqeJRSmnlNx367FTwiLXjLFMN_9Ilpjjcln1Dk_SiXOErMYvOXeFMmFt8aAQqsNUY353rWyYWzUIyU264y8rV5it8F_LPf3g7-BQAA__9cF4Fr) this winter. In the past 100 years, there have only been 13 documented sightings. Which city in each state fits the Hallmark movie mold—snow-dusted streets, glowing storefronts, and just enough small-town charm? On today’s ___[Flyover Podcast](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFugzAURb8GNpD9DBgGD1VbVKlJlalNs1gGPxKrDiD7JYR-fZX2Ezrd4UrnSOdjq7fGeTce38wZFd2GSY-4RI9EGDR67Cm43tEaDGG8zEdnRhq8BgZlxiEDoa1xftVFalXHbMNTVFxWVdFwwSE9qcLWfVMNta1lCZ0FWQFUBmTTiZqxrk-dusM4gODAeMlzAUPHRA0VNqUUgiUFQ-toCjmdkPBm4uDX6Yoh76dz6tWJaI6JeEigTaBdliVfpwtdOrz_CbSzN6t3kRLR_s3TbvP8Hj_39Lrzj7vD90sb9jLbzIegv9iYcSubNCg7L857FyuZFOx4Ns7_-uh_la4KfgIAAP__vvJ7KA)_ , Ayla Brown breaks down a new study highlighting the towns most likely to inspire a real-life Hallmark holiday story. [Tune in here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFugzAURb8GNpD9DBgGD1VbVKlJlalNs1gGPxKrDiD7JYR-fZX2Ezrd4UrnSOdjq7fGeTce38wZFd2GSY-4RI9EGDR67Cm43tEaDGG8zEdnRhq8BgZlxiEDoa1xftVFalXHbMNTVFxWVdFwwSE9qcLWfVMNta1lCZ0FWQFUBmTTiZqxrk-dusM4gODAeMlzAUPHRA0VNqUUgiUFQ-toCjmdkPBm4uDX6Yoh76dz6tWJaI6JeEigTaBdliVfpwtdOrz_CbSzN6t3kRLR_s3TbvP8Hj_39Lrzj7vD90sb9jLbzIegv9iYcSubNCg7L857FyuZFOx4Ns7_-uh_la4KfgIAAP__vvJ7KA). **Just Eight Days Left in 2025—And a Decision That Matters** There are **just eight days left in 2025** , and our current _Flyover_ funding round is**set to close** next month. More than **60% of the round is already subscribed** , and once it’s filled, this opportunity will be gone. We don’t want our most loyal readers to look back and realize they meant to participate—but missed the window. As we head into 2026, **a bigger question** we are asking our readers is: **Are we really going to enter another year with the same tired, biased, politically motivated legacy media?** More celebrity journalists chasing clicks.
More opinions disguised as reporting.
More political agendas shaping coverage instead of facts. Or are we going to charge ahead and make a meaningful change? _The Flyover_ was built on a simple but increasingly rare idea:
**Fact-based reporting, no political agenda, no spin, no outrage—just common-sense news for common-sense Americans.** Changing how millions of Americans get their news doesn’t happen automatically. It happens when readers step forward—not just to read, but to [own and help build something better](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unDAQhZ_GdIvwGEMoXETa0G1us1LSWV48wETGRmbYn7ePSDZS-tueb-bTzPlxsRdHgeL03S1o-DkmG_GxBWTGbDHgwJkG4ld2jNu-TuQij8FCBfok4QTKekfhZevCm1vlO1mgkW3T1J1UEorZDFK5tq61rPXoWvQeRiVV14JuQPvmVpA5ZBJASaiklqWC8VapL9Bgp1ulKlFX6IlTLnlGxqfbxvBKd8zlkJYimJl53YT6KqAX0FO848blr0SRZ_xv8sCq33mxW9rzgEKdv3liSlFAc8QLetoXoc4fj4j-dEFP7o0Gt6yOpijUuf9r_AdSZIx8LF37d8aYD8v1Z_9hj7dAF9n49UEh0Na0oq6mxVH4cz1_rvO7gd8BAAD__78dlpc). ## Mexican Navy Aircraft Crashes Near Galveston
A Mexican Navy aircraft on a medical mission [crashed yesterday afternoon](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2upDAQhE8DmZHd5mcIHGxC9jbdEHns9tCSwcjuGeD2K-YKLy6pvqrv38_8YynS9vprVzR8hjRveJSIzJhnjOg4kyO-smUs7_1FduMQZ5DQCQUC9OwtxWtua2-e0o-qRqOGvm9HpRXUixlVPzw6PaqHs-C8lkPrB4X6qfXou-BqMneZAtAKpOpUoyE8pX5Aj2M3aC2rVqInTrnhBRlPW0K80gdz49JaR7Mw76XSfyqYKpiO42hCOu8Xd17B9C4VTOs7Mu0RRbBsIzFhERn3lBm9WPEkZzex2c8l9mg3FC7bsmARX55wyRYWGd2NvQSGkDLX2fj9oBip9EPVytdqKX438e9Mfgz8DwAA___MTYut) near the Galveston Causeway. Two passengers were rescued, while five have been confirmed dead, including a two-year-old child. One remains missing as of press time. Eight people--four naval officers and four others--were aboard the King Air ANX 1209 when it crashed during its approach. The flight was sponsored by the Michou y Mau Foundation, an organization that helps children with severe burns [receive advanced treatment](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOwyAQAL8Gd0Sw-BEXFNe4y7VXWhivHaQ1INjEyd-fcr9w9Uij0fzc5psLFOL-7Q60_NrSHPGshMxYZiT0XIIP_C6OsT7yHlzkjWZQ0EkNEsy8ukDvuW1Wu6h11A1aPfR9O2qjoblbpfTgh7Zvu75Dj7i0nV_GoV-UG822XJtgPzINYDQo3emLgW1R5go9jt1gjBKtwjVwKhe-I-PL1Y3e6Ynl4tPRkL0z5yrMl4BJwOQWr82HCJhyqixgOh7EIRNKt2P0AassWHOKqyyYU-EqM7mIck1nlKdjLHJ39MTKKQqY9NWowWgQMDXFrvkMRKH2g2jVfrhAfxn8v3lPC78BAAD__6Flg8w) at Shriners in Galveston. The U.S. Coast Guard and the Galveston County Sheriff's Office responded to the incident, deploying dive teams and drones for the search and rescue effort. The FAA, Texas Department of Public Safety, and Mexican Navy are investigating the crash. Fog conditions [limited visibility](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE9jMiO7DWYIHGxCNptuiAy0oSVjEO6F4fZfzBV-VlJJr1Tv37t_e4qU5r9-RcefsPUJrxyRGY8eI4580Eh8H54x_99n8olD7EFBLTVIMP3kKd59VUxuUFOrC3S6sbZqtdFQLG4ItTaTDdCqxmCw9esVGgSrrRostqYg98A0gNGgdK1LA2FQ5gUW27oxRolK4US8HSUvyPjxOcR7O_Eox20toluY9yzMHwGdgO66rnIc8vPi6QV0TxTQrfih0SeZ_HnLPfqEcjx8XuTs44mZtyS_cLniRKOPcqWcaUsCuuJw035RjJRtIyo1r57id51_5-x08BMAAP__peqCVA) to half a mile at the time of the crash.
## Electricity Rates Up 30% Since 2021
Average electricity rates in Texas have [increased by 30%](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGOqzAQANDTmC7IHtsQChe_octvt0QDHpORHLDMbBJuv8peYS_wivd1m27Imbf1Pz4oyDvt00avI5MI1YkyLVJ5YTkrCh3fZWXcJOUJNPiLgQvYKSLnc3JNDLOOg2komL7r3GCsgeYeUvIu9Tj45Hs3k7vaOHTWJkAdcfGx4fDBDIA1oI03rYU0a3uFjgbfW6uV0xRZ9trKnYTeeKR87k-q7bI_mhzuIuVQ9p-CUcEoVCq3e10VjB9YwWhAwYgp7TXizJnlLFioKhibGmJ5cc58dL1yen0g519W_pbxDPATAAD__1w_cm8) since 2021, and they're projected to rise another 29% by 2030, according to a recent study by the Texas Energy Poverty Research Institute (TEPRI). The organization highlighted large-scale investments in transmission and distribution as the primary driver of increased rates. "_That’s how they get paid_ ," [said Sandie Haverlah](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0T3PnDAMwPFPE7acEucFGBgqVWxP147IgMNZzR0omIe7b19xU_eutvSXfvbvr-ELOfNz-YUP6uSV1uFJ555JhMpAmSYpPLG8Cwrtx7YwPiXlAQwEbUGDG2bk_B58NXejmVtbUWfrGH1rnYXq3hkkTCOE2AZLM2F0vvGjic0YJtP6VHF3xSyAs2BssDcHaTSugUhtqJ0zyhuaWdZykzsJvXBP-b1-U7lN66PK3V1k25X7oaBX0J_neTsT4rVU0GMRnjIp6C-Xgj6vE2YF_Sek_xHqD1HvR1n4ueiED858DaQcy3LdSMuq_xBt-tgU9NDUGshbB9Zq8iFp72qvG4NBR9sEk0YMc4jK9YWScj_pxaILTVymqnTzdnLOvMdaebM8kPOHI__3hO8O_gYAAP__KBSacg), president of the Texas Consumer Association. "_Build a new line, get paid. Put in more equipment, get paid_." Power grid operator ERCOT has projected that peak demand could [reach 150,000 megawatts](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkTGvnDAQhH-N6XzCaw5CQZGG7qVNidZmza1ibGQv745_H92LlD_wmi1Wo29mNL8_lg_kyGn7hTtN8gp5SfSskUSoLBTJS2HPchUUquexMSYJcYEW7tqABrusyPFaumadXLuOpqHJDH3fjcYaaB6T61s3enDOEMLgYOyd8cZ3Fu4DdkgNT2-YAbAGWnM3NwvBtfYH9DTeB2tb1bW0suRykwcJvbCGeOVPKjef9yZOD5GjKvtTwaxg9nnfz8Ry8X6gl7dGwYxnFU4K5ppPeWhPSQpG_f_9hdWRNq4R5SykYH7HUjAb8z6gYKZEZbs0vQ4qUvVZNtJVUEhL1oL-TyR95CcVjSHksqLjyHJpCoE9U_KXxqr_WW0lP6uCuSnTejw5Rq79oLp225HjVzH53hyfE_wNAAD___Fdouk) by 2035. At last month's Texas Energy Summit in Austin, consultant Doug Lewin attributed the growth to industrial users, such as data center and cryptocurrency mining operators. Earlier this year, TEPRI found that 65% of low-income Texans [engaged](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOpDAQAL8GMpDd5nEEDi4hm0svRA1uMy31YGR6hmG_fjX7C5uXqqT6f5tuyMLb-g8f5PUd07TReQipUp5IaNHMC-uVUel47ivjplEmMNBWFipwU0CWa2rK4GcTBluSt33XNYN1Fsq7b3EwhHMf2zhgA8uMwUVnrQMzh-BCyf4jswDOgrGtrR3E2bg_0NHQ9s6ZojEUWFOu9U5KbzyiXOlFuV7SoxR_V92Pwv0tYCxgPM-zDum5Cp28fYgCxr2AcRb84m2tNCPLUcWUK9oor1eFMaYccGZhvcrsw36yCB9dXzRmfSDLT0h_t-fl4TsAAP__NlN7zQ) in "energy-limiting behaviors," such as turning off their air conditioning or heating to save money on their electricity bills. "_This is a threat to public health_ ," said Lewin. "_This is not okay. We need to solve this problem_."
## Texas Tech Expanding Weather Forecast System
State lawmakers allocated $24 million to Texas Tech University to [expand](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL_GdCB7bexQuLiGLtdeiQxeB0tLQGYvTv7-lPzC1aOZ0Xs_1-kaMuX77Tts6PmZ9umO9SRkxjIh4cIlL5lfJTCev8cthzsnmkBC3ypoQU8xZHpNpol-lnFQDXrlrDWD0gqa1aO02hll3DyDcba_LEnDMNtLQieljk327zEFoBVI1atOQ5qlvoDFoXdaS2Ekxsx76XhFxmc4E732B5Zu2beG_Mp8nEJ_CRgFjLXWbiv8zgSMbxgBYyicF0IB46ffMi5rWzHwiqVNtO-xBQU9DNZ1x3o0xcejZqJ8WieMvG0h0-eN_-fo4eEvAAD__3mseio) an advanced weather forecasting system in the aftermath of this summer's flash floods. The project aims to equip decision-makers with real-time data, enabling them to determine the best course of action during severe weather events. Over the next two years, a team of professors plans to add [more than 40 weather stations](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2ypCAUxfHVYIYFFz-eAcEkZj3phBYNF6UKhYJr2-5-qnsLbwO_c-r_77E8TIjhWP-aHTW9fVoOvGpEIiwLRrRUgg10F0NYz7wGc5CPCwjouQQOanEmxHvpGqefwk2yQS3HYegmqSQ0mwZrn2h6q7of4YQbrfAdWDkNRnb9NPVN0B9MAigJQvayVeCfQv3AgFM_KiVYJ9AFSqWlDQnfpvp4pxeW1qa9iXojypWpPwxmBvN1XS3R2aI7GcxHuhjMOVWqDObPEINZAoP5C3FCu_FcksdaU6mcEnf4wpgyr2QIuU_2rOi4jyk57lNBayqFY-X1roR7m7fcFO3yFWIMdRhZJ9bdhPg9R79L-tLwPwAA__9n2ozB) to the 25-year-old West Texas Mesonet (WTM), a network of over 170 such stations west of I-35. Automated rain gauges and high-resolution radars will also be installed in strategic locations. The WTM's expanded data collection capacity will enable the development of a highly specific forecast up to two days in the future by running 100 simulations four times a day. "_With all those new measurements, we're augmenting and reducing the uncertainty of what the starting point is for that system_ ," [said Professor of Atmospheric Science](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LvOnDAQBeCngc7IHrNcChdp6DZtSuTLGKx4MbJnYXn7iFXe4C9HGn065_x5zk8dYtiW3_qFij4-zRueJSIR5hkjWsrBBrqyJizvfQl6Ix9n4PBgAhjI2ekQr7mtnTLcjaJGJfqua0chBdSr0sK0Y288B-P9IEZpBuhH36P3XSuGrg7qxgSAFMDFQzQSvOFygA7HRy8lr1qOLlDKDa1I-NHFxysdmBubXnVUK9FeKvmrgqmC6TzPBg_MF61hW-LbmGT_3p8VTHe1CqaYrI7s__EFGaFd2Z6Tx1JSLszhgTHtrJAmZD7Zd0HHfEzJMZ8yWl0obAsrVyG86Tort58hxlC6vmr58tIhfgPSz2Y9FPwLAAD__3akkIA) John Schroeder.

As we head into 2026, a bigger question we are asking our readers is: Are we really going to enter another year with the same tired, biased, politically motivated legacy media? More celebrity journalists chasing clicks. More opinions disguised as reporting. More political agendas shaping coverage instead of facts. Or are we going to charge ahead and make a meaningful change? The Flyover was built on a simple but increasingly rare idea: Fact-based reporting, no political agenda, no spin, no outrage—just common-sense news for common-sense Americans. We’d love to welcome you as an owner as we head into 2026. | Mexican Navy Aircraft Crashes Near Galveston A Mexican Navy aircraft on a medical mission near the Galveston Causeway. Two passengers were rescued, while five have been confirmed dead, including a two-year-old child. One remains missing as of press time. Eight people--four naval officers and four others--were aboard the King Air ANX 1209 when it crashed during its approach. The flight was sponsored by the Michou y Mau Foundation, an organization that helps children with severe burns at Shriners in Galveston. The U.S. Coast Guard and the Galveston County Sheriff's Office responded to the incident, deploying dive teams and drones for the search and rescue effort. The FAA, Texas Department of Public Safety, and Mexican Navy are investigating the crash. Fog conditions to half a mile at the time of the crash. Electricity Rates Up 30% Since 2021 Average electricity rates in Texas have since 2021, and they're projected to rise another 29% by 2030, according to a recent study by the Texas Energy Poverty Research Institute (TEPRI). The organization highlighted large-scale investments in transmission and distribution as the primary driver of increased rates. "That’s how they get paid ," , president of the Texas Consumer Association. "Build a new line, get paid. Put in more equipment, get paid." Power grid operator ERCOT has projected that peak demand could by 2035. At last month's Texas Energy Summit in Austin, consultant Doug Lewin attributed the growth to industrial users, such as data center and cryptocurrency mining operators. Earlier this year, TEPRI found that 65% of low-income Texans in "energy-limiting behaviors," such as turning off their air conditioning or heating to save money on their electricity bills. "This is a threat to public health ," said Lewin. "This is not okay. We need to solve this problem." Texas Tech Expanding Weather Forecast System State lawmakers allocated $24 million to Texas Tech University to an advanced weather forecasting system in the aftermath of this summer's flash floods. The project aims to equip decision-makers with real-time data, enabling them to determine the best course of action during severe weather events. Over the next two years, a team of professors plans to add to the 25-year-old West Texas Mesonet (WTM), a network of over 170 such stations west of I-35. Automated rain gauges and high-resolution radars will also be installed in strategic locations. The WTM's expanded data collection capacity will enable the development of a highly specific forecast up to two days in the future by running 100 simulations four times a day. "With all those new measurements, we're augmenting and reducing the uncertainty of what the starting point is for that system ," John Schroeder. | Flying together with our sponsor When 14,000 investors show up before Wall Street You rarely see a private company cross 14,000 investors before Wall Street even clocks what’s happening. , with more than $60M raised, recurring 7-figure contracts in place across major brands and agencies, and a reserved Nasdaq ticker, $RADI, while still private. This isn’t a concept-stage AI story. RAD Intel’s platform is already wired into real marketing budgets, helping brands decide where the next dollar performs best before they spend it. That edge is starting to show up in the business. . The company’s valuation has increased by more than 5,000% in approximately 5 years. Backed by multiple Fidelity funds and VCs, and selected by the Adobe Design Fund, RAD Intel attracts early investors—including operators from Google, Meta, YouTube, and Amazon—who spot inflection points early. Through a qualified Reg A+ offering, . On one side, you have traction that reads like an earnings call. On the other hand, a price that still lives in an early-stage window. Gaps between performance and price like that don’t usually stay open for long. | ➤ Marlin: The town's volunteer fire department purchased its first-ever new fire engine with funding from the Texas A&M Forest Service and the local emergency services district. The $402,000 pumper tanker can transport 3,000 gallons of water. () ➤ Bexar County: Two deputies were shot while responding to a domestic disturbance call on Sunday. The officers, who are expected to recover, killed the suspect when they returned fire. () ➤ Houston: Texas EquuSearch has joined the effort to find Sydney Marquez, a 24-year-old El Paso woman who was last seen in Houston's Asiatown on the night of Dec. 11. () ➤ Dallas: Demetrius Rogers, 18, was arrested in the fatal shooting of a 28-year-old man at a DART station last Thursday night. Rogers and his brother reportedly said "excuse me" to the victim as they passed him and his children, to which he replied, "excuse you." () ➤ Laredo: A 308-foot flagpole near the Juárez-Lincoln International Bridge was removed Friday. It was installed in 2002 at the former David B. Barkley Plaza, which was replaced with a motor bank that has since been abandoned. () ➤ Waco: Judge Dianne Hensley, a justice of the peace who was disciplined by the State Commission on Judicial Conduct for refusing to officiate same-sex weddings, has filed a federal lawsuit seeking to overturn Obergefell v. Hodges , the 2015 Supreme Court decision that legalized same-sex marriage nationwide. () ➤ Dallas: The upscale Preston Hollow neighborhood is restricting access to confirmed residents and guests of the community to limit traffic associated with one homeowner's viral Christmas lights display. name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Have you checked your texts, Peter?
body:
It feels like I’m talking into the void!

| | | |
Golly, Peter!
Judging by the effort it took, you’d think I was attempting a world record just to get your eyes on this!
[For heaven’s sake, all I’m asking is that you read this message:](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.) | [ READ MARSHA’S MESSAGE >> ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)
---
\- Marsha Blackburn

# **Share This Post!**
I can’t thank you enough for your support as I move forward with this campaign.Without grassroots funding from those like you, we would never be able to strive for better representation and those that want to fight for the 8th district. With that being said, I just have a quick favor to ask of you: campaigns like mine aren’t just about fundraising, we also have a clear message we want to send to the country and to the constituents of the 8th district. Can you share our message with this link below and get the word out about this campaign?
| [](https://1xz3r.mjt.lu/lnk/AWoAAIn0kaIAAc6txigAA-Neh6MAAYKKXNoAoUWGADIMFwBpSpRA-I6xVGqhQgiWKbJY2hUH7QAtq28/2/IWNpowgW1N4-f2jsTgmAaw/aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL3RoZXktYXJlLW5vdC1saWtlLXVz)

Rooted in faith, we look forward to the next decade of defending truth, preserving freedom, and honoring God in all we do. To our dedicated community of medical liberty supporters - parents, advocates, and freedom fighters - who work with us to protect and advance informed consent, medical privacy, and vaccine choice: may your homes be filled with joy and your hearts strengthened by our collective victories. **Merry Christmas from Team TFVC**

name: =?UTF-8?Q?Eduardo_Ver=C3=A1stegui?=
subject: Friend, your love can bring a child home for Christmas
body:
A child should fall asleep on Christmas Eve listening for reindeer hooves...

| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e80912/VaHR0cHM6Ly9vZGIubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvWkFiQlU2dVJoOQ/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsckdQdTVNeQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNqa1l1dndMc1czdjBscw/gaUqXxw/s4ueb702016)
| | [](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
Friend, A child should fall asleep on Christmas Eve listening for reindeer hooves... **Not to footsteps in the hallway outside a locked room.** **Right now through December 31, your gift to the Sound of Freedom Foundation will be MATCHED—dollar for dollar—so you can do DOUBLE the good for God’s children.**
---
* **$25 = $50**
* **$50 = $100**
* **$100 = $200**

---
But this opportunity ends **at midnight on December 31.**
---
[ **Double My Gift Before December 31** ](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
My name is **Eduardo Verástegui**. I produced the film _Sound of Freedom_ , and I now serve as President of the **Sound of Freedom Foundation**. I want you to know: **I've given my life to fight for God’s children who are trapped in the nightmare of sex trafficking.** Right now, while families are hanging lights and wrapping presents, there are children who don’t even know what day it is. They are far from home. No tree. No lights. No family. Only the constant terror of being used, bought, and sold by adults who see them as a product — not as sons and daughters. Some parents are staring at an empty seat at the table this Christmas.
A stocking that will stay limp.
A bed that’s made... and cold. That is the brutal reality of child sex trafficking. And this Christmas, I’m asking you to do something beautiful and bold: **help bring a child home for Christmas.**__
---
[](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
If you watched _Sound of Freedom_ and thought, “I wish there were something real I could do,” this is that moment. Because a movie is not enough. We needed a mission.
---
We launched the Sound of Freedom Foundation to:
* partner with trusted investigators, law enforcement, and aftercare teams to help **locate and rescue children

**
* help build the evidence needed to **arrest and prosecute traffickers

**
* support survivors with **safe shelter and trauma-informed care** so they can begin to heal.

---
All of this flows from one unshakable conviction... **God’s children are NOT for sale.** And here is something I need you to understand: we cannot do any of this without people like you. Every investigation we help fuel...
every safe bed prepared for the moment a child walks free...
every trauma counselor who sits with a survivor as the nightmares spill out...
every step toward healing and restoration... All of it depends on someone deciding: **“Not on my watch. Not this Christmas.”** That’s you. When you give, you step into the story as the hero. We are simply the guide—connecting your courage and generosity to children who need it most. And right now, your compassion goes twice as far. [**Give before December 31 and your gift is matched dollar for dollar.**](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
[](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
[ Double My Gift: Bring a Child Home ](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
I want you to picture a moment with me. A child has been rescued. For the first time in a long time, they sleep in a safe bed. They wake up to soft light instead of harsh neon.
Someone tells them, **“You’re safe now. No one here will hurt you.”** Behind the scenes, a team is working—confirming identities, contacting authorities, and searching for family members who have been praying, weeping, and waiting. Sometimes it takes months.
Sometimes years. Then one day, in an airport or at a border crossing, a door opens... a mother sees the child she thought was gone forever... there are no speeches, no cameras—just tears, shaking shoulders, and a whispered, **“Thank You, God.”** **This is the kind of miracle your generosity can help make possible.** Not every reunion happens exactly on December 25th. But the heart of Christmas—**God stepping into our darkness and bringing His children home—is what drives everything we do.** Traffickers don’t take holidays off.
So we can’t either. That’s why I’m asking you—urgently and personally—to make your year-end gift **before midnight on December 31** , while it will still be doubled.
---
[ Yes, Double My Gift Today ](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
Because somewhere, right now, there is a child who feels utterly forgotten. Your gift is a message that whispers into that darkness: **“I see you. I’m fighting for you. I’m helping bring you home.”** **[Will you help bring a child home for Christmas?](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)**
---
With gratitude, Eduardo Verástegui
Sound of Freedom, Producer
For the Sound of Freedom Foundation
---
[](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
P.S. The match ends**December 31 at midnight. Please give now so your gift will be doubled.**
---
[ Support the Sound of Freedom Foundation ](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)
---
6671 Southwest Freeway, Suite 700, Houston TX 77074
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **Free Thinker Daily**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e80912/VaHR0cHM6Ly9vZGIubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsckdQdTVNeQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNqa1l1dndMc1czdjBscw/gaUqXxw/sbre31b26c2)

Because somewhere, right now, there is a child who feels utterly forgotten. Your gift is a message that whispers into that darkness: “I see you. I’m fighting for you. I’m helping bring you home.” With gratitude, Eduardo Verástegui Sound of Freedom, Producer For the Sound of Freedom Foundation P.S. The match endsDecember 31 at midnight. Please give now so your gift will be doubled. 6671 Southwest Freeway, Suite 700, Houston TX 77074

Peter, this is personal to me. My parents immigrated to the U.S. from India when I was a baby to give us a better life — and they did.
I went from living in Public Housing and depending on SNAP to eat — to a Law School graduate, U.S. Congressman, and candidate for Senate.
The Trump administration’s cruel, xenophobic policies mean that so many immigrant kids like me are scared right now.
**So yes — I’m angry. And I’m worried. **
But if I’ve learned anything in my life as an immigrant in this country, it’s that success comes from translating anger and worry into action.
**That’s why I’m running for Senate: to fight with _everything_ I have against Trump and his MAGA maniacs. **

clean_body: These emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. If you'd prefer, you can . = = My name is Raja Krishnamoorthi. The attacks on immigrants across the country are despicable. As an immigrant and the father of first-generation brown children, I refuse to stay silent. I’m doing everything in my power to protect the families and children living in fear that their parents will be ripped from their homes, schools, jobs, and communities by ICE. From standing up to Kristi Noem in the House Oversight Committee to getting kicked out of ICE detention centers for doing my job, I’ve led the fight against Trump’s authoritarian agenda. Democrats need someone willing to fight in the Senate now more than ever. But that’s made me a target for Republican attacks = | | = Peter, this is personal to me. My parents immigrated to the U.S. from India when I was a baby to give us a better life — and they did. I went from living in Public Housing and depending on SNAP to eat — to a Law School graduate, U.S. Congressman, and candidate for Senate. The Trump administration’s cruel, xenophobic policies mean that so many immigrant kids like me are scared right now. So yes — I’m angry. And I’m worried. But if I’ve learned anything in my life as an immigrant in this country, it’s that success comes from translating anger and worry into action. That’s why I’m running for Senate: to fight with everything I have against Trump and his MAGA maniacs. = | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for standing with me, Raja Krishnamoorthi | Democrat Raja Krishnamoorthi is running for Senate in Illinois to fight back against Donald Trump and the far-right MAGA movement, protect critical programs like Social Security and Medicare, and rebuild the middle class. name: Commander-in-Cheat
subject: Trump is laying the groundwork to steal the 2026 midterm
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Lauren Boebert is key to Trump’s ambitions to lie, cheat, and steal his way to permanent power.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001d5D1sY_GK33THSPSxHqsFcAql6eYpALp43nHhsZRQaQbSa31dJ7nxDyCecaIvPWNv0YRyK0k_rDAcs5cGNcFBswolqwxyjZ-7wb9cekWbHVZ2k9VcdRdR7mBbnqOGjYFt65eiMItQqRzwzQVc4flVrqjH501HSdGyZoLTR7nv3hYdUuwmxlqc1e0y256LeI-3LuZTILG5xrO0bNCi5tMg8e5uBxcSXU6&c=YRq7n7BK21vUanAyQsOIeIRAy0Ua_Vft6Ggd0Qu-Xf6-BB0SlA0tHw==&ch=ew4c0p5OixeoD8_jTrZdbF4BnAcGZ--MxPwn_H4BHLg2iRTZtpSNFw==)
---
|

Peter,
Donald Trump falsely claims that convicted felon and election-tampering former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters "did nothing wrong."
In fact, it was a Republican prosecutor who brought charges against Peters before a jury of her peers. She was convicted and sentenced to much less than the maximum time she could have gotten in prison.
Trump tried to pressure Governor Jared Polis to release Peters. When Polis refused, Trump called him a "sleazebag" and threatened "harsh measures" against Colorado.
Trump then tried to pardon Peters for state crimes, which the president cannot do.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001d5D1sY_GK33THSPSxHqsFcAql6eYpALp43nHhsZRQaQbSa31dJ7nxDyCecaIvPWNv0YRyK0k_rDAcs5cGNcFBswolqwxyjZ-7wb9cekWbHVZ2k9VcdRdR7mBbnqOGjYFt65eiMItQqRzwzQVc4flVrqjH501HSdGyZoLTR7nv3hYdUuwmxlqc1e0y256LeI-3LuZTILG5xqbdWHsRLThbiqY_32Tnxkk&c=YRq7n7BK21vUanAyQsOIeIRAy0Ua_Vft6Ggd0Qu-Xf6-BB0SlA0tHw==&ch=ew4c0p5OixeoD8_jTrZdbF4BnAcGZ--MxPwn_H4BHLg2iRTZtpSNFw==)
---
What is really going on here? It's simple: Trump knows that to encourage supporters to commit crimes on his behalf, he has to exonerate every MAGA felon that supported his Big Lie.
Tina Peters tampered with voting machines and sensitive election data in an effort to "prove" Trump's nonsense claims that the 2020 election was fraudulent. MAGA is now watching to see if Trump will fight to get her released from prison.
Lauren Boebert is key to Trump's ambitions to lie, cheat, and steal his way to permanent power.
That's why Boebert's Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, urgently needs your support right now. Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, as her Democratic opponent, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts as Boebert underperformed Trump in every single county.

clean_body: Lauren Boebert is key to Trump’s ambitions to lie, cheat, and steal his way to permanent power. | | | | | == | Peter, Donald Trump falsely claims that convicted felon and election-tampering former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters "did nothing wrong." In fact, it was a Republican prosecutor who brought charges against Peters before a jury of her peers. She was convicted and sentenced to much less than the maximum time she could have gotten in prison. Trump tried to pressure Governor Jared Polis to release Peters. When Polis refused, Trump called him a "sleazebag" and threatened "harsh measures" against Colorado. Trump then tried to pardon Peters for state crimes, which the president cannot do. | == What is really going on here? It's simple: Trump knows that to encourage supporters to commit crimes on his behalf, he has to exonerate every MAGA felon that supported his Big Lie. I wanted to reach out one more time as we approach the end of the year, before everyone settles into the holidays with family and friends.
This has been an important year for our campaign. We proved just how strong this movement is, and we showed that Northwest Ohio is ready for new leadership. As we close out the year, this final fundraising deadline matters. It allows us to start the new year on solid footing and keep building the grassroots effort that's carried us this far. Continued support now helps ensure we don't lose momentum heading into the months ahead.

subject: Trump started this fight. We’re going to finish it.

body:
Democrats CANNOT surrender and lay down our arms while MAGA fights dirty for complete control.
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0011ChEnztBxG14hwynOw0eTv2pQ_4vjT_Yqsz_iAw2cJOY7mLtkkXJQRaT0E1aUnyaPodLQgzcz0XQ6ACxe7KLyRrEewYc5J4v8JKxhp8rT2mz0OhX4KkEuZmLgUJjywdhYY8YKb3vgUAUr0As2e3pFZYtHOEfEg_D2V9dncjA5ryQeXjWcggpGZfwz4945EBy26P0H3cCBsQFd3sw5OMa39xwYLfRrFY6&c=hkNJ2SOJulfOiFoMOrf5gr2MnY0KyjITzknJuL8wG-Q-u0YIx3GUpg==&ch=XuT06_aGh7Zu0DavISNyLjoAYVp2j-kx1pO3288-k15KMHR8xmB-zQ==)

Peter,
We know you have better things to think about than "mid-decade redistricting."
But Donald Trump started this fight to try and rig the Midterm elections before votes are even cast. Now, we're going to finish it -- and make Trump regret ever underestimating us.
Here's the honest truth: we support independent nonpartisan redistricting commissions. We need campaign finance reform and honest regulation. Political gerrymandering isn't a good thing.
But Democrats CANNOT surrender and lay down our arms while MAGA fights us with brass knuckles to take complete control of the House for generations.
A brighter future for our country depends on us waging this battle with everything we've got.
A half-assed effort to fight back, strongly worded press releases, and mere performative resistance will not do.
We must commit completely to countering Trump's election rigging. Anything less would betray future generations of Americans who won't get to experience freedom and democracy.
That's why Colorado Draws the Line is leading the charge to introduce a ballot initiative for Colorado voters to approve a new congressional map that could net Democrats as many as three GOP-held seats.
Trump is so deadset on winning this fight, he is threatening even red states and GOP legislators if they refuse him. That's what we're up against. And we can only win this battle with your help.
[Will you please join this critical fight and help our team deliver the knockout blow to Trump's plan to rig the election by making a donation of $50, $25 or $5 today?](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0011ChEnztBxG14hwynOw0eTv2pQ_4vjT_Yqsz_iAw2cJOY7mLtkkXJQRaT0E1aUnyaZSsniHlEEIOPz35VDG6OxqAZwMNlsezrh5eW1r7Yy5qr6Al4AOuqYYN52UapT2F14A1x2XM8Qck0cEK7ExijA6zQWzm_VmeZtjTFBnEnTb5F3bYUd2eMGoYF8hywl6uve3UtPL2gHuBis_wUGFzNVUcijXJ6jy1scmFojZwM5GA=&c=hkNJ2SOJulfOiFoMOrf5gr2MnY0KyjITzknJuL8wG-Q-u0YIx3GUpg==&ch=XuT06_aGh7Zu0DavISNyLjoAYVp2j-kx1pO3288-k15KMHR8xmB-zQ==)

clean_body: Democrats CANNOT surrender and lay down our arms while MAGA fights dirty for complete control. | | | | | == Peter, We know you have better things to think about than "mid-decade redistricting." But Donald Trump started this fight to try and rig the Midterm elections before votes are even cast. Now, we're going to finish it -- and make Trump regret ever underestimating us. Here's the honest truth: we support independent nonpartisan redistricting commissions. We need campaign finance reform and honest regulation. Political gerrymandering isn't a good thing. But Democrats CANNOT surrender and lay down our arms while MAGA fights us with brass knuckles to take complete control of the House for generations. A brighter future for our country depends on us waging this battle with everything we've got. A half-assed effort to fight back, strongly worded press releases, and mere performative resistance will not do. We must commit completely to countering Trump's election rigging. Anything less would betray future generations of Americans who won't get to experience freedom and democracy. That's why Colorado Draws the Line is leading the charge to introduce a ballot initiative for Colorado voters to approve a new congressional map that could net Democrats as many as three GOP-held seats. Trump is so deadset on winning this fight, he is threatening even red states and GOP legislators if they refuse him. That's what we're up against. And we can only win this battle with your help. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == Thank you for fighting back. -- Colorado Draws the Line | | | | == | | == | Donate by mail: Colorado Draws the Line == == | Paid for by Colorado Draws the Line. I’m the daughter of an immigrant farm worker who later served as a police officer after returning from combat in Vietnam. Never in my wildest dreams did I imagine that middle-class grit would lead me to Congress, or that I’d become the first Latina ever elected to represent Oregon. But that path started with Emerge.

**I didn’t just run as a candidate. I ran as a mom. And moms know how to show up prepared, focused, and determined. Emerge saw that in me right away and said, “You belong here, and you can win.”**

They didn’t just teach me how to campaign. They gave me a sisterhood that carried me every step of the way — phone calls, late-night strategy sessions, encouragement on the days when doubt crept in.

And now, in Congress, I see every day why electing more women — especially women of color — isn’t just important. It’s essential. Representation changes what gets fought for, who gets protected, and whose stories shape policy.

Right now, there are 13 Emerge alums serving in Congress and next year, Emerge has a chance to double that number. That’s the kind of power shift we need if we’re going to stand up to Donald Trump’s agenda and actually protect our communities.

[_**Emerge is trying to close a $50,000 year-end gap so they can train the next class of women candidates — women who will flip seats, build coalitions, and lead with the grit and preparation I know so well. Will you donate before December 31 to help them meet this goal?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121834096/590734979/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-12-23_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIwYTk0OWFhLTA5ZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=hUGv7ZUranQU_Xyf9xz9W9CMHc7LrsagPK31wfgx4qg=)

clean_body: They didn’t just teach me how to campaign. They gave me a sisterhood that carried me every step of the way — phone calls, late-night strategy sessions, encouragement on the days when doubt crept in. | = | Peter, I’m Congresswoman Andrea Salinas from Oregon, and I want to tell you why = | = I’m the daughter of an immigrant farm worker who later served as a police officer after returning from combat in Vietnam. Never in my wildest dreams did I imagine that middle-class grit would lead me to Congress, or that I’d become the first Latina ever elected to represent Oregon. But that path started with Emerge. I didn’t just run as a candidate. I ran as a mom. And moms know how to show up prepared, focused, and determined. Emerge saw that in me right away and said, “You belong here, and you can win.” They didn’t just teach me how to campaign. They gave me a sisterhood that carried me every step of the way — phone calls, late-night strategy sessions, encouragement on the days when doubt crept in. And now, in Congress, I see every day why electing more women — especially women of color — isn’t just important. It’s essential. Representation changes what gets fought for, who gets protected, and whose stories shape policy. Right now, there are 13 Emerge alums serving in Congress and next year, Emerge has a chance to double that number. That’s the kind of power shift we need if we’re going to stand up to Donald Trump’s agenda and actually protect our communities. = | | | = | = | = | = | = Emerge gave me the gift of believing I could run and win and the tools to make it real. Your support ensures the next generation of women gets that same opportunity. —Rep. Andrea Salinas Emerge Alum & U.S. Representative (OR-06) = * | | = Paid for by Emerge America Emerge America 818 Connecticut Avenue NW Suite 450 Washington, DC 20006 United States | = =

## Commonsense Corner:
---
Tim wrapped up ringing the Salvation Army bell outside The Cheesecake Factory yesterday afternoon, and it was another simple reminder to pause and think about others this time of year.

A lot of people are hurting, sometimes in ways we can’t even see. And while politics has a way of dividing so many of us, life is too short not to do good whenever we can—even in small ways.

We were talking with Tim after his bipartisan prayer group last week, and he shared how important it is to follow your heart when God puts someone on it. Pick up the phone and make that call or send a handwritten Christmas card.
Taking a minute or two doesn’t seem like much, but it can make a real difference for people during the holiday season. You may even change someone’s life.

body:
Thank you for being a part of our team.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121837384/590746769/-153530995?refcode=EM_C_251223holidays_N_SB_90DO_3_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxODAwZDllMS0wYmUwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OFJmCDQcmyi4rFoKoBzieiUL4zNEKsD_kaWETjSFOG0=) Peter,

Before we take a short break, we wanted to wish you happy holidays!

I am so grateful to continue to serve as Congresswoman of Ohio’s 11th District. It is an absolute honor to be in the position to continue fighting for the American people with you by my side. As we prepare for critical midterm elections next year, we know that we have a lot of work ahead of us. But I know that together, we are up for the battle.

**Thanks for standing with us, and happy holidays to you and your loved ones.**

Thank you,

Shontel Brown | Shontel Brown is proud to be elected as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District. She has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. As she enters her second full term in Congress, she will use that experience to continue the fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and to preserve and protect our democracy for generations to come. Will you join her and be the change we need?

As many Americans celebrate Christmas Eve tomorrow, hundreds of thousands of brave service members are deployed in harsh conditions, facing unforgiving weather and the uncertainty of escalating global conflicts.

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | I laid my eyes on and immediately put it in my wife's purse You don't want your wife to have to look over her shoulder at night Neither do I. name: Lizzie Fletcher
subject: Wishing you the happiest of holidays
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
This holiday season, I am especially mindful of the many blessings we share—and I want you to know that you are one of them. Since 2017, I have had the honor of working with so many people committed to strengthening our country and improving the lives of others near and far.
From my neighbors across TX07 and throughout Houston to my colleagues in Congress, I am grateful for all those who are doing their part to build a better, brighter future. This season of joy and light gives me the opportunity to reflect on that work and the privilege I have in doing it. I am deeply thankful to the people of TX07 for trusting me to be their voice in Washington, and I am grateful to you for the support that makes this work possible.
As we look toward 2026, my commitment to defending our democracy, advancing equality, and restoring our rights remains steadfast. The challenges ahead are real—and the choices we make will shape our community and our country for generations to come. But I am ready for the work ahead, and I am honored to do this work with you.
From our home to yours, Scott and I wish you and your loved ones the happiest of holidays.
Lizzie Signature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]
3262 Westheimer, PMB 636, Houston, TX 77098
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS

name: Abdul for U.S. Senate
subject: 315 lbs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1460%2E251025%2EX0FVWo ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1460%2E251025%2EX0FVWo ]Abdul for US Senate

Peter, you may
know Abdul is a doctor, a dad, and a lifelong fighter for working people.
But did you know he can do this?

[ Abdul ]Abdul lifting 315 pounds

This summer, Abdul went viral for benching 315 lbs like it was nothing. 🏋️

As he likes to joke, the real heavy lifting isn’t in the gym — it’s taking
on the would-be oligarchs and corporate PACs who value profits over
people. And for a fight like that, everyone needs a spotter.

That’s where you come in,
Peter.

In just a few days, we’re facing a major FEC fundraising deadline.
Everything we’ve raised to that point will be made public for the press,
pundits, and even our opponents to see.

If our numbers are strong, we prove that a fully people-powered,
pro-Medicare for All campaign can win this Senate race.

If they’re weak, the establishment will use it as an excuse to write us
off.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/691?c0=em-ms-aes-20251223-1-ak.JjOQZo&refcode=em20251223-1455&t=1004&akid=1460%2E251025%2EX0FVWo ]So please, pitch in $3.15 or more today to help us show the strength of
our movement leading up to our FEC deadline. 💪

Thanks,

Team Abdul

body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251223-935&t=1&akid=944%2E294397%2EXh5UYJ)
Would you believe us if we told you even **_$1.00_** can make an impact?
It’s true! Let us explain how...
As you may already know, we’re preparing for a surge of far-right billionaire spending in this race for the Wisconsin Supreme Court.
**Right-wing special interests have poured millions into Wisconsin before — and they’re ready to do it again to try to tip the scales in their favor.**
But we have something they’ll never have: You and other members of this team.
Grassroots support is how we overcome the onslaught of dark money. It’s how we protect our fundamental rights. It’s how we’ll elect Judge Chris Taylor next April.
And right now, this support matters more than ever.
**On December 31, we’re facing a public fundraising deadline.** Every dollar raised by then will be visible to our opponents and the right-wing special interests backing them.
That’s why investing early _— even if it’s just $1.00 —_ keeps our momentum going.
[**With that being said, can you chip in $1.00 or more to help us stay ahead?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/154?c0=em_ms_jct_20251223_1_ak.9eAk0R&refcode=em20251223-935&t=3&akid=944%2E294397%2EXh5UYJ)

Thank you,
Team Taylor

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

**NASA obsessively locked away Armstrong’s secret tape—knowing the fallout could be explosive.**
[ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=360ebb18d464410e80e59079876883a9&_e=AoJlA-z2zDRyVWiVbZaCCPLaduJnA-h2Z7cJ_IRcw-VeEuCMQT0SRTavQnE7fbvqjFuUQr8637Xzv2i83vqgMA-SvIf1Okry6WO9ldBvjlQwr5fe2tviN74VU1YwZaOetLzS3usV-gonreNWBOhW0ge4QjkT4lPs4KsrmQeCiBa15g0lkxChNBdyq8GQIja9x-n2rVG5AHs9PSD1Lh7RiDk2dMZqv4YnMiXCzCYY3SSBlCTlEI1jU7kIFDLJGb0nFHq0C8LYhb5F2xf5fylXyX0Ua5N8rSywHvAbHJk0AOqKUXicyF0PYOfCBf2kpexsAxDlSTOmHd0Dh1IKTWif3gg0MESkCo0I_9PV8flIvtOJwwJYsiAAqErBagEf765vqR0i__3llocWGR1N35AHD3xj_07MOzZrBjE2k3o1YWoWwCZofGYdXP8KYL3zxU9KjAaszbo8rERX9OKL19JFyrk1ZXmi2RBlcbG-uE0MBz6NVa7c--g2KiHX0q5stoSgDONvJkV7wLHG6pjn-gAlAGRzepGGiX4TSpTmeZe-u__5LGD9HZBBvsw9F5nv2sv5-Z8XAZUxmtwO1pTYwNZeWw%3D%3D)
Against all odds, the hidden recording has been revealed, carrying Armstrong’s anxious warnings and shattering NASA’s carefully built defenses.

This urgent audio sparks global panic, rewriting the story of space exploration in real time.

The consequences are snowballing—trust devastated, reputations on the line, and a wave of uncertainty sweeping the world.

[Don’t miss the alarm-inducing truth before the authorities make one last attempt at lockdown.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=360ebb18d464410e80e59079876883a9&_e=AoJlA-z2zDRyVWiVbZaCCPLaduJnA-h2Z7cJ_IRcw-VeEuCMQT0SRTavQnE7fbvqjFuUQr8637Xzv2i83vqgMA-SvIf1Okry6WO9ldBvjlQwr5fe2tviN74VU1YwZaOetLzS3usV-gonreNWBOhW0ge4QjkT4lPs4KsrmQeCiBa15g0lkxChNBdyq8GQIja9uzck2iTLJk_qsrLF_pa7a83_9Jt8ZqYy49tMxRvuDlDYpjVxBvmf-uIGk_GhMYEmPy2yEPxkvZPXIfrxuas0HRi9q5aJFFrC8Zy1qPt9imCUYuvCuuagljb_MqZImBcr8q0Y7OpcgkObzUSvV9l8xzm_4wLIPmgTfeBqD6jEc9bW0w6zEDwLDuP3uYy7kqLStrljjJ1cROtq1EbYpQWG1gleiT72OYc8s6I7k6VnetJ4mpOt4hfQX0NmWTMBjWb8gSRatz00mYyxAZnZ_YOR5uGJtBjJNepaawefiUoao5CTLAYMBNEMYncPKy0AwQ_Asyk28tgO3_bGMyMvzuB5jbMXjyzLQwSzbqkm75yz4LoozbGu9Cio_4cg8TalkcriygkzvndDMK9QoepNAo52Qg%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: NASA obsessively locked away Armstrong’s secret tape—knowing the fallout could be explosive.| | | | December 23 | | | | | Suppressed Armstrong Tape—NASA’s Secret Crisis Exposed | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
