clean_body: Trump and his allies lash out at me because they need a scapegoat. Friend — After the last month, it shouldn’t surprise you that Trump attacked me with personal insults and threats to deport me once again this weekend. JD Vance joined the fray this time too, referring to Minneapolis as “Mogadishu” in a racist joke for his far-right buddies. Randy Fine added his two cents on the right-wing TV channel NewsMax, saying that I “shouldn’t be an American” and that Somali-Americans “need to go home, starting with her.” Let me explain. Trump and his allies lash out at me because they need a scapegoat. They need a distraction to direct attention away from their failures. They tell hardworking Americans that the reason they can’t get ahead is because of immigrants and Somali people. That’s a much easier sell than explaining to your supporters that you are kicking them off their healthcare, skyrocketing the cost of living, slashing their food assistance, and lining the pockets of billionaires at their expense. But rhetoric eventually meets reality. Nobody thinks about Somali-Americans when they see their energy bill has doubled due to Trump’s AI data center expansions. Nobody is going to think of my name when they see their insurance premium double after the GOP’s budget cuts, and have to decide to go uninsured. Americans are smart enough to know when the President is screwing them over. It’s our job and the job of this entire progressive movement to continue to push people toward that fundamental truth, and rally behind a positive message for an affordable America where immigrants are recognized for their contributions to our prosperity, not scapegoated. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your contribution will go through immediately: | | ** | | | | | | | In solidarity as always, Ilhan Omar PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS

name: Phil Weiser
subject: Friends, our teachers and students deserve better
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Phil Weiser for Colorado
body:
My plan for Colorado’s public schools

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121856035/590799303/1239926708?refcode=ea.86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjE5NmE3MC0xZmUwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ww3Nfz7gnqr8gqCRojOt-C2c5khl_8qGsJC5EzRMD_w=&emci=abfbc970-51df-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=51844520) Friends, it's Phil. This campaign is about listening — and working together with the good people in Colorado to deliver solutions. That’s why it is important for me to share what I am hearing from Colorado teachers, students, parents, school board members, school administrators, and community leaders. It's a wake up call. **We are losing teachers, and our students cannot succeed without them.** At a recent roundtable, I heard from Jefferson County teachers that over 50% of them worked second jobs. All of them worried that they could never afford to buy a home. And they were all struggling to afford health care. In fact, these teachers aren’t alone. In 2025, nearly half (47%) of Colorado teachers revealed they are considering leaving the education profession in the near future, citing heavy workloads, burnout, high stress, and low pay. Like the one featured on Time Magazine’s cover below, teachers’ personal stories are _harrowing_. No teacher should need to work extra jobs, rely on food banks to make ends meet, struggle with medical debt, or find themselves unable to afford a home.**I have a plan for us to do better — read on to learn more.** ******Case study: Adams 12 School District**
Adams 12 School District, north of Denver, is a case in point on how we can lean in and do better. In early 2025 the District announced it needed to cut nearly $30 million and 150 jobs for the 2025-2026 school year due to budget pressures. Salaries for its teachers already rank 10th out of the 15 nearest districts. To address these challenges, in 2024, Adams 12 leaders had pushed a new county funding ballot measure. That one failed; yet they kept at it. This Fall, a similar measure passed, thanks to a grassroots educator-led effort including teachers knocking on doors, improving teacher pay, offering mental health support to students, and supporting critical staff like bus drivers and cafeteria workers. The question before us: _will we make these critical investments across our state?_ **The Trump Administration’s attack on public education**
The Trump administration is making matters worse, undermining public education by threatening to withhold critical funds, cutting teacher training, and slashing youth mental health funding. _**We are fighting back. I have gone to court to protect Colorado in each of these cases.**_ The latest challenge is pressuring Colorado to accept a school voucher-type program. I welcome more federal support for education and investing in our students’ future. _**But I will never support privatizing our public K-12 schools**_ — or siphoning off scarce public education dollars to private and religious institutions. **Instead, my Colorado Blueprint calls on us to invest in K-12 education by:** 🍎 **Paying** our teachers a living wage
🍎**Providing** down payment support to teachers, so they can afford homes in the communities where they work
🍎 **Working** with Colorado teachers to create better options for health insurance
🍎 **Leading** an effort at the ballot box so we can invest in K-12 schools, teachers, and our kids’ futures **[How do you think we can improve our community’s public education system? Let us know here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121856036/590799304/959330636?refcode=ea.86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjE5NmE3MC0xZmUwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ww3Nfz7gnqr8gqCRojOt-C2c5khl_8qGsJC5EzRMD_w=&emci=abfbc970-51df-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=51844520)** | | [LEAVE A SUGGESTION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121856037/590799305/959330636?refcode=ea.86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjE5NmE3MC0xZmUwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ww3Nfz7gnqr8gqCRojOt-C2c5khl_8qGsJC5EzRMD_w=&emci=abfbc970-51df-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=86196a70-1fe0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=51844520)
---
**As Governor, I will make improving Colorado’s system of public education — including supporting our teachers and our students’ futures — a top priority. I am ready to and committed to taking on this important challenge. To help me do so, please share your ideas. I will read them all and follow up with more details on how we can do better.**
With you for our teachers, students, parents, and communities,
Phil
|
---

Hey Peter, it’s Andy Beshear. **Today, I’m reaching out to ask you to join me in supporting Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign in Georgia.** I’ll explain more in just a moment, but if you already know the stakes, you can chip in to help keep Jon in the fight: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ossoff-em-dec25**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251223-1-f&t=2&akid=1329%2E5691347%2EMCbL3d) Look, I know a thing or two about how to win as a Democrat in the South. _And it’s not easy._ In my first election for Governor of Kentucky, the odds were stacked against us. Donald Trump had won the state a few years prior by _almost thirty points._ Right-wing Super PACs spent a fortune to try to defeat me. But thanks to the support of an incredible grassroots team and an extraordinary effort, we shocked the nation — and we won. So, take it from me: Jon Ossoff’s Senate race in Georgia will likely be the most competitive and expensive election in the nation next year — _and winning won’t be easy._ The GOP and their Super PACs will spend hundreds of millions of dollars to try to defeat Jon and send another Trump lackey to the United States Senate. Winning this race means Jon and his team will need to build the largest statewide campaign Georgia has ever seen to fight back and win. They’ll need more volunteers, more ads on the air, and more staff to have a decisive victory next November. Running a campaign of that size takes an unprecedented level of resources, so Jon needs supporters like you to join him and help fuel his efforts _right now._ **[That’s why I’m asking: Can you join me in supporting Jon Ossoff by making a donation to his reelection campaign? Every dollar counts in a race like this.](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251223-1-f&t=3&akid=1329%2E5691347%2EMCbL3d)** **[CONTRIBUTE HERE](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251223-1-f&t=4&akid=1329%2E5691347%2EMCbL3d)** Thank you. \- Andy Beshear

body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121857693/590804912/334489679?&refcode=E16938-20251223-AgerSurrogate-Image-Surrogate&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk4MjcyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImVkZTc1MTExLTIxZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=DZHiow6ti1KjvDKotLRP2oLgnsCfdQeaGcdIv2KrCic=) Hi Peter, **My name is Jamie Ager, and I’m running for Congress in North Carolina’s 11th District. I’m honored to be endorsed by the Blue Dog PAC this cycle.** I’m a fourth-generation farmer, a father, and business owner. I helped build a sustainable meat company that now supports 25 employees and partners with dozens of family farms across our region. For my entire career, I’ve worked to help families put food on the table. [_**Now, I’m running for Congress to fight for small businesses, family farms, and working people. This seat is flippable and if we win, we can help take back the Speaker’s gavel from extremists in Washington D.C. But I can’t do it without you. Will you chip in $5 or more to help power our campaign today?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121857694/590804914/334489679?&refcode=E16938-20251223-AgerSurrogate-SL-Surrogate&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk4MjcyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImVkZTc1MTExLTIxZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=DZHiow6ti1KjvDKotLRP2oLgnsCfdQeaGcdIv2KrCic=) [DONATE $5 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121857695/590804915/334489679?&refcode=E16938-20251223-AgerSurrogate-SB-Surrogate&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk4MjcyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImVkZTc1MTExLTIxZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=DZHiow6ti1KjvDKotLRP2oLgnsCfdQeaGcdIv2KrCic=) Public service runs in my family. My grandfather served in Congress and helped shape early programs like Medicare and Medicaid. My father served in the same State House seat that my older brother represents today. **So when I watched my Congressman, Chuck Edwards, vote to cut Medicare just to give another tax break to billionaires, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. Western North Carolinians deserve better.** [_**If you’re ready to flip NC-11 and elect a Congress that actually works for working people, I need your help today. clean_body: | | = Hi Peter, My name is Jamie Ager, and I’m running for Congress in North Carolina’s 11th District. I’m honored to be endorsed by the Blue Dog PAC this cycle. I’m a fourth-generation farmer, a father, and business owner. I helped build a sustainable meat company that now supports 25 employees and partners with dozens of family farms across our region. For my entire career, I’ve worked to help families put food on the table. = = Public service runs in my family. My grandfather served in Congress and helped shape early programs like Medicare and Medicaid. My father served in the same State House seat that my older brother represents today. So when I watched my Congressman, Chuck Edwards, vote to cut Medicare just to give another tax break to billionaires, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. Western North Carolinians deserve better. = If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your payment will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- Thank you, Jamie Ager Candidate for Congress, NC-11 * If you would like to receive fewer emails, we understand. to sign up for only the most important updates, or you can . name: Dan Goldman
subject: Trump’s abuses of power impact all of us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, Donald Trump is using the office of the presidency to benefit himself and his allies while targeting those who oppose him. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858133/590806294/1749249761?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251223_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTA5Y2RkYy0yMGUwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x1rCMyqdv1c48Ad1l-PBvGtHYfC5lX1J3xxyyGjJ4zY=) Trump has issued over 1,800 acts of clemency in his time as president, more than 1,600 of which have been during his current term. The vast majority of his pardons? The violent insurrectionist criminals who invaded the Capitol and assaulted more than 170 police officers on January 6th. And a number of those violent insurrectionists have — not surprisingly — reoffended, and no doubt stand ready to inflict violence at Trump’s beck and call. But it’s not just the violent rioters from January 6. Trump is doling out pardons to those who invest in his crypto fund, contribute to his campaign, or just happen to be MAGA, like George Santos, the disgraced former lawmaker who was convicted of multiple fraud schemes. **Meanwhile, as he saves his cronies with pardons, he is using the Department of Justice to try to put his political enemies in jail.** The despicable abuse of presidential power is _staggering_. Despite the obvious abuses, neither Republicans in Congress, the Supreme Court, nor the Department of Justice leaders have even tried to hold him accountable. So Peter, that means**it’s our responsibility to hold his administration accountable and protect everyday Americans from his dangerous agenda.** [**Trump’s abuses of power pose a grave danger to our Constitution, the rule of law, and our democracy. Please stand with me in fighting back and speaking out by making a contribution of $10 or more today:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858134/590806295/1749249761?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251223_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTA5Y2RkYy0yMGUwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x1rCMyqdv1c48Ad1l-PBvGtHYfC5lX1J3xxyyGjJ4zY=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ |

clean_body: | Peter, Donald Trump is using the office of the presidency to benefit himself and his allies while targeting those who oppose him. = Trump has issued over 1,800 acts of clemency in his time as president, more than 1,600 of which have been during his current term. The vast majority of his pardons? The violent insurrectionist criminals who invaded the Capitol and assaulted more than 170 police officers on January 6th. And a number of those violent insurrectionists have — not surprisingly — reoffended, and no doubt stand ready to inflict violence at Trump’s beck and call. But it’s not just the violent rioters from January 6. Trump is doling out pardons to those who invest in his crypto fund, contribute to his campaign, or just happen to be MAGA, like George Santos, the disgraced former lawmaker who was convicted of multiple fraud schemes. Meanwhile, as he saves his cronies with pardons, he is using the Department of Justice to try to put his political enemies in jail. The despicable abuse of presidential power is staggering. Despite the obvious abuses, neither Republicans in Congress, the Supreme Court, nor the Department of Justice leaders have even tried to hold him accountable. So Peter, that meansit’s our responsibility to hold his administration accountable and protect everyday Americans from his dangerous agenda. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you, Dan Dan Goldman for New York PO BOX 3306 New York, NY 10008 United States | mailto:info@dangoldmanforny.com | Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York

name: Matias, United We Dream
subject: Our members eat, sleep, and breathe this work
body:
| | | Peter, In September, Arlo stood on a stage in front of 10,000 people at a rally and spoke a truth that captures everything about this moment: _"You and your comunidades matter. Your story matters. You matter."_ Arlo joined United We Dream just last year as a freshman in college, looking for community and connection. Today, they're lobbying Congress. Leading campus organizing. Standing alongside movement leaders they once looked up to from afar, holding hands, chanting for a country free from authoritarianism and federal agents disappearing our friends and family. But Peter, this year hasn’t been easy. Immigrant youth are more vulnerable than ever — not just because of the uncertainty of DACA’s future, but because of the growing hostility targeting our communities. **Immigration agents are snatching immigrants in our communities, throwing them into detention without any form of due process. Budget cuts stripped healthcare from our communities. In Texas, undocumented students lost access to in-state tuition this year.** The attacks aren't slowing down. Next year, members will continue defending to protect community members, fight budget cuts, and organize on campuses nationwide. They'll do it because, as Arlo says, they eat, sleep, and breathe this work — not just as organizers, but as people whose families, friends, and themselves are being targeted. Arlo puts it this way:_"UWD has shown me that my humanity doesn't need to be separated from my organizing. Transformation and radical change begin when we bring our identities and stories to the table, and when movements are rooted in radical love."_ **That's what your support makes possible** — a space where young people don't just organize, but where they laugh, cry, dance, and practice radical joy together. Where they learn they're capable of leading campaigns and winning. Where movements are rooted in love, not just resistance. __ [**If you believe in empowering young people to organize boldly and build the future we all deserve, please make a donation before our End-of-Year deadline. During these uncertain times, every dollar makes a difference.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvvSptAA5-xC0ZLMh3sw_H8hyCmECnODiQzfTWOA-yBF31WdUIDoJ5iAdtAR0gm8E8fmxmls9pag-pQ_ITkxfa7w_f6nBmB733Iks0TQtc132skRD108QKJdXzbJWQ0zq4fB1Ht3diF5r08lVxyG-Hl9Qufe2g4OEGAdlxJKDTiIxJLxEXbOKJuLQOtPFY8jVqGNJwniAuna3TCo8ElAPDyK1KBvLdM626lW70aoYPl7-fDkJCQQabBLO4CF6yJyXYCLdM8zZfdSrzcgPtmGjNU82Y6KvyrL7Rdm-4vYwYhXh4L6uL1kbbrOgnMGaE0VY-LmVEF1iymbwZlDH2JYzrZHQFXBqVFN1wjpQlrqiT0ef1Ev4kMJK4AGe6M1Mt-S-WFsnB-mCs8aczmgJUF7YlGIaBPg7_yPv4qY3VKBtoUTJ2Qu1eM9BefG67veyIquc8/4mo/v94MuxlCTzGsxtg0lha4xA/h0/h001.caYRCjHpeU7lNmKh29K92JdYc9DbATMn7mAfcpW161U) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [express donate: $25](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvvSptAA5-xC0ZLMh3sw_H8hyCmECnODiQzfTWOA-yBF31WdUIDoJ5iAdtAR0gm8E8fmxmls9pag-pQ_ITkxfa7rMMvv1rphZ3jRObdRL8vCOaK1wSMU1qe9h1twYfF2AflT7UfJAlJ2iyQTazWjyCxnjw8Oab7sV_WFLNbCu1YO6WJB6ShMWvqN753_K8U_cU_iVDnSPBjXMbabf9byfv-qvAHhmpZMWIXgB2Lq5IqMXv9OLp89h4G7IdGYUjzWbIPy0yGf_xTqGxyNSZbtjolkDdiKq250k7O-y8y1fr7soF20RmBea9Of7kTgL2lgKixktfjaZqWGvvNXIHZkV8C7OSR2phHeE1nIuh-QQczlkEnItgKCHzg0kOSEtHNsIRTk8lYHGiXktjeYi58pLI7UVcghTY50LFS-N8cEuRcYsRUXq6X4DjQjzJ1bCxYC7cG3pWZEJTGtUQfMkl-keECmrxR7f9J0OLWGr9OIhYQNw/4mo/v94MuxlCTzGsxtg0lha4xA/h1/h001.cp7IDl94b9koPMFOca29VqQwRLtCD1HI8YT0dvQl2J4)

clean_body: | | | Peter, In September, Arlo stood on a stage in front of 10,000 people at a rally and spoke a truth that captures everything about this moment: "You and your comunidades matter. Your story matters. You matter." Arlo joined United We Dream just last year as a freshman in college, looking for community and connection. Today, they're lobbying Congress. Leading campus organizing. Standing alongside movement leaders they once looked up to from afar, holding hands, chanting for a country free from authoritarianism and federal agents disappearing our friends and family. But Peter, this year hasn’t been easy. Immigrant youth are more vulnerable than ever — not just because of the uncertainty of DACA’s future, but because of the growing hostility targeting our communities. Immigration agents are snatching immigrants in our communities, throwing them into detention without any form of due process. Budget cuts stripped healthcare from our communities. In Texas, undocumented students lost access to in-state tuition this year. The attacks aren't slowing down. Next year, members will continue defending to protect community members, fight budget cuts, and organize on campuses nationwide. They'll do it because, as Arlo says, they eat, sleep, and breathe this work — not just as organizers, but as people whose families, friends, and themselves are being targeted. Arlo puts it this way:"UWD has shown me that my humanity doesn't need to be separated from my organizing. Transformation and radical change begin when we bring our identities and stories to the table, and when movements are rooted in radical love." That's what your support makes possible — a space where young people don't just organize, but where they laugh, cry, dance, and practice radical joy together. Where they learn they're capable of leading campaigns and winning. Where movements are rooted in love, not just resistance. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | Together, Matias, United We Dream | Follow us on: | | | | |

name: Alex Kelloff
subject: These last seven months
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alex Kelloff for Colorado
body:
https://alexkelloff.com/ [https://alexkelloff.com/]Greetings,
As we near the close of another year, I’ve been thinking a lot about gratitude. Long before this campaign, my family used to place messages in the local paper at the end of the year just to say thank you.
They didn’t announce any big sales or promotions, it was their way of showing their appreciation to their customers and their community. That spirit has stayed with me.
https://alexkelloff.com/ [https://alexkelloff.com/]
As the year winds down and we all gather a little closer to the people who matter most, I’ve been reflecting on what these last seven months on the campaign trail have meant to me.
It’s hard to capture it all, but “grateful” is the word that keeps coming back to me. Meeting families across the district and listening to your stories has been one of the most rewarding chapters of my life.
Thank you for showing up time and again. Your encouragement, your patience, your feedback, and your faith in this effort have meant more to me than I can say.
I hope the holiday season brings you rest, warmth, and time with those who lift you up. And as we look toward a big year ahead, please know how deeply grateful I am to have you on this team.
Wishing you and your loved ones a peaceful and joyful holiday season.
With gratitude,
Alex

name: Marlene
subject: my family’s flan recipe
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858411/590807457/-718111328?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.23_Flan_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_RS_T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTNmZjM1OGQtMjFlMC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=EtVVY_vjF2BgaHlz_r5ixgBM4xh9NgLVjcBM_aGsTmA=&emci=9e81a223-41dc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=13ff358d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=626985)

Folks, I want to tell you a story that begins long before my name was ever on a ballot. My parents were young and scared when they fled Cuba for the U.S. They left everything behind — their traditions, their language, their loved ones — because they believed in a simple, powerful dream: _That their children could grow up in a country where freedom was real, where opportunity wasn’t reserved for a lucky few, and where democracy belonged to the people._ Every holiday season, my mother and I made flan together — a recipe she carried with her. In our family, it’s more than a dessert. It’s a reminder of our roots. **Now, I’m in charge of making flan for my family every holiday season.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858419/590807465/1302948222?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.23_Flan_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_RS_T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTNmZjM1OGQtMjFlMC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=EtVVY_vjF2BgaHlz_r5ixgBM4xh9NgLVjcBM_aGsTmA=) As I run for Congress to represent AZ-01, I’m fighting to ensure that the country my parents trusted with our future continues to live up to that promise. If you believe in this mission, I’m asking you to chip in whatever you can to my campaign today. [**_Donate any amount (even a dollar!), and I’ll share my family’s flan recipe with you:_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858424/590807472/1302948222?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.23_Flan_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_RS_T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTNmZjM1OGQtMjFlMC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=EtVVY_vjF2BgaHlz_r5ixgBM4xh9NgLVjcBM_aGsTmA=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [ CHIP IN $5 NOW >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858427/590807476/1302948222?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.23_Flan_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_RS_T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTNmZjM1OGQtMjFlMC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=EtVVY_vjF2BgaHlz_r5ixgBM4xh9NgLVjcBM_aGsTmA=)

clean_body: | | Folks, I want to tell you a story that begins long before my name was ever on a ballot. My parents were young and scared when they fled Cuba for the U.S. They left everything behind — their traditions, their language, their loved ones — because they believed in a simple, powerful dream: That their children could grow up in a country where freedom was real, where opportunity wasn’t reserved for a lucky few, and where democracy belonged to the people. Every holiday season, my mother and I made flan together — a recipe she carried with her. In our family, it’s more than a dessert. It’s a reminder of our roots. Now, I’m in charge of making flan for my family every holiday season. = As I run for Congress to represent AZ-01, I’m fighting to ensure that the country my parents trusted with our future continues to live up to that promise. If you believe in this mission, I’m asking you to chip in whatever you can to my campaign today. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = | = Merry Christmas! – Marlene Galán-Woods | To learn more about Marlene, visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Marlene for Congress 7904 E. Chaparral Road, Suite A110-472 Scottsdale, AZ 85250 United States

name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
The President of the United States threatened my life, personally.
| | |
---
Hey there, it’s Mark Kelly.
Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate.
I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy.
But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something.
So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too.
I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help.
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.
After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do.
**_[I’m asking you to split a donation between Michael Bennet and me, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858494/590807801/1446759855?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1089-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNmZmMzU4ZC0yMWUwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HYd7ntPFeF9CFJPjER8YY481cgQtFRVqn5PubtrcZYc=)_**

We have a major opportunity to elect a proven climate champion in Texas next month and deal a devastating blow to the fossil fuel industry. **Our JanePAC-endorsed climate champion Christian Menefee is heading into a critical runoff for Texas’s 18th Congressional District on January 31 after emerging as the top finisher in a crowded 16-candidate field.[Now he’s counting on grassroots supporters like you to help him cross the finish line. Can you chip in any amount directly to his campaign to help him secure this seat and kick off 2026 with another major victory for our climate movement?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858544/590807881/615746269?refcode=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3MGRjNjFkLTIxZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xx9_UJADWq1MiE2-z6haH5g1qyro5QdNMgjCOB-T1LU=)** Here are three reasons why this election, _and your support,_ couldn’t be more important: **1️⃣ This is the first electoral fight of the 2026 midterms.** A win in Texas will shrink Trump’s Republican majority in the House and give us another climate champion in Congress who can help block Trump’s fossil fuel giveaways. **2️⃣ Christian has a proven record of standing up to big polluters.** As Harris County Attorney, he's taken on Big Oil and won, cutting emissions and expanding clean energy access in communities poisoned by industrial pollution for decades. TX-18 deserves a _principled, fearless leader_ who knows how to take on powerful interests and win. **3️⃣ Runoffs are won by campaigns with early and consistent grassroots support.** Turnout is low. Margins are tight. Every dollar we send Christian helps him reach voters who may not even realize this election is happening. Your contribution could be the one that pushes him over the finish line. I'm asking one last time before the new year: **[Will you chip in directly to Christian's campaign and bring us one step closer to flipping the House blue in 2026?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858550/590807890/615746269?refcode=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3MGRjNjFkLTIxZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xx9_UJADWq1MiE2-z6haH5g1qyro5QdNMgjCOB-T1LU=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858556/590807928/615746269?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3MGRjNjFkLTIxZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xx9_UJADWq1MiE2-z6haH5g1qyro5QdNMgjCOB-T1LU=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121858562/590807949/615746269?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.c70dc61d-21e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM3MGRjNjFkLTIxZTAtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xx9_UJADWq1MiE2-z6haH5g1qyro5QdNMgjCOB-T1LU=)

clean_body: Are you in? | We have a major opportunity to elect a proven climate champion in Texas next month and deal a devastating blow to the fossil fuel industry. Our JanePAC-endorsed climate champion Christian Menefee is heading into a critical runoff for Texas’s 18th Congressional District on January 31 after emerging as the top finisher in a crowded 16-candidate field.= Here are three reasons why this election, and your support, couldn’t be more important: 1️⃣ This is the first electoral fight of the 2026 midterms. A win in Texas will shrink Trump’s Republican majority in the House and give us another climate champion in Congress who can help block Trump’s fossil fuel giveaways. 2️⃣ Christian has a proven record of standing up to big polluters. As Harris County Attorney, he's taken on Big Oil and won, cutting emissions and expanding clean energy access in communities poisoned by industrial pollution for decades. TX-18 deserves a principled, fearless leader who knows how to take on powerful interests and win. 3️⃣ Runoffs are won by campaigns with early and consistent grassroots support. Turnout is low. Margins are tight. Every dollar we send Christian helps him reach voters who may not even realize this election is happening. Your contribution could be the one that pushes him over the finish line. I'm asking one last time before the new year: = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you for stepping up, xx Jane = | Follow along: | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

Unlike Ramaswamy, I'm a life-long public servant.

I worked hard to put myself through college and med school, and I became a preventive medicine and pediatrics doctor. After a career working to improve community health, I joined Gov. DeWine's administration as the director of public health, where I helped lead Ohio through an unprecedented public health crisis.

**Ohioans deserve a leader who will place their interests first. And this poll shows that we're well on our way to making our case across Ohio.**

But it's going to be a tough fight to break ahead of Vivek Ramaswamy and win this race – but I know with your support, we can do it.

clean_body: | = Peter, I want you to be one of the first to see a new poll that shows me leading Vivek Ramaswamy by ONE point: = It's clear as day that this is a winnable race, Peter. **= | = Unlike Ramaswamy, I'm a life-long public servant. I worked hard to put myself through college and med school, and I became a preventive medicine and pediatrics doctor. After a career working to improve community health, I joined Gov. DeWine's administration as the director of public health, where I helped lead Ohio through an unprecedented public health crisis. Ohioans deserve a leader who will place their interests first. And this poll shows that we're well on our way to making our case across Ohio. But it's going to be a tough fight to break ahead of Vivek Ramaswamy and win this race – but I know with your support, we can do it. =** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Be well, Dr. Amy Acton = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Team Demand Justice PAC
subject: Billionaires are trying to buy 2026
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251223-61019&t=1001&akid=61019%2E1407376%2Ekgjisd ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251223-61019&t=1002&akid=61019%2E1407376%2Ekgjisd ]Demand Justice PAC

The Supreme Court is considering a case that could tear down limits on
campaign spending, and Republicans are pushing it hard.

If they win, Super PACs, special interests, and outside groups could pour
unlimited money into our elections, drowning out the voices of everyday
voters. Wealthy donors could funnel money through political parties,
essentially buying politicians and shaping policy to serve their own
interests.

This is a direct power grab designed to rig the 2026 elections in favor of
the well-connected few.

This is about who has the power to make the rules, who gets heard, and
whether Americans can still have a voice in our democracy. We can’t let
our elections be bought and sold by billionaires.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251223.GyAHC2&refcode=em20251223-61019&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61019%2E1407376%2Ekgjisd ]Take a stand today. Chip in $5 to demand the Supreme
Court protect our campaign finance limits and keep our democracy
accountable to the people, not the ultra-wealthy.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251223.GyAHC2&amount=5&refcode=em20251223-61019&t=1022&akid=61019%2E1407376%2Ekgjisd ]Donate $5 ››

In solidarity,

Team Demand Justice PAC

body:
Every Who in the US liked Christmas a lot. But the Trump, who ruled over the US, did not. | | | [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9TYM2ujKOIJdCIDub-bHyeZ0r9V0YqSHsZ-a23c8_6RG-lR2zcmPHiYoW2cWTclXdREdyrJx5OhIwaCnIkjJS2hyTSVx2VmsLGm9A8kEhWZFo14XMb2pa-UeRq-bx7bRvmXYOquP6fFdp77k1DDUcQu8jjuHJFHA3MBKpsijnyIwrn8Q4CQw8gwW9-4QOubTAHAdrbNL_8g_5RcRpNPM92ufuwmwe-S2cc0zA0tVSeYeJW92oTGgO4JpwCH4PQlq2mlxdgD_KXcKeLqqzCe-vf6EaVjTmK1g3dDSODwXdxFASDbfAylwMpkn5-orDHqkcoiHcozfHxgNLrIzPLFk3mI1tNJDikKMtTT77iiWQ9Xv5eZr_1jGgTlzZ6VxQPrwi/4mo/4c1E_dVMT0mHy_to0FSMHg/h0/h001.9aaAsdKhlHJh163m9ytavPV3y4QUA6MiKyBLdc8OnY8) **The Trump Who Stole Christmas, A WFP Story** _ Every Who in the U.S. liked Christmas a lot. But the Trump, who ruled over the U.S., did not. The Trump HATED Christmas! The whole Christmas season! Now, please don't ask why, no one quite knows the reason. But I think that the most likely reason of all, may have been that his heart was two sizes too small. "I know just what to do!" The Trump laughed in his throat, and he cancelled the aid that keeps families afloat. And he chuckled, and clucked, "What a great Trumpy trick! Without SNAP or insurance, the suffering starts quick!” Then he slithered and slunk, with a smile most unpleasant, raising prices with tariffs on every present. Oh, the billionaires with their tax cuts, they laughed — campaign bribes always work on a man who's so daft. Now, the problem today is that unlike the Grinch, the Trump will not change. His heart won't grow one inch. The Whos need us to stop him and his Trumpy friends. Fighting back is the way that the suffering ends. As we enter the new year, we know what to do: We'll make our country work for the many, not the few. **[So I'm asking, dear reader, right now if you can, to please donate $10 to help our team plan.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9TYM2ujKOIJdCIDub-bHyeZ0r9V0YqSHsZ-a23c8_6RG-lR2zcmPHiYoW2cWTclXdoOKhCdmZEUAitzJJyUud4ltlua_gGrG1EFEf0Qev8HCGs1WoUG4WqyqKiI60rlw7e08qwc4w8Hfmmg5xuEJ4Ujm6TgW6TyP6P1ENhu7GnLTu3Wp7M9Og6n_pxAo7QqAoJ2mCQJdWt_JH5h5h7E90UXZ8My-6nTyFoeLV__b3ztR3a22uKDgZl4ZZdkPzzXiCsFJ1kv0uHL39EbKAwyaqevHq1llB9wFvDxsUMIobjf__QCS35jR51Pgr-prcTeku994oNlcG2oulsfxp9LqHpJL88tGxcBSpWMe3CmVc-Y0sp7PxIyP8BgnPMQtbMey-/4mo/4c1E_dVMT0mHy_to0FSMHg/h1/h001.MWRXgztgje00JkXNnsMTXrUMY0JE0_DdXFgYWC5utbs)** Your support means the world as we fight to be free. clean_body: Every Who in the US liked Christmas a lot. But the Trump, who ruled over the US, did not. | | | The Trump Who Stole Christmas, A WFP Story Every Who in the U.S. liked Christmas a lot. But the Trump, who ruled over the U.S., did not. The Trump HATED Christmas! The whole Christmas season! Now, please don't ask why, no one quite knows the reason. But I think that the most likely reason of all, may have been that his heart was two sizes too small. "I know just what to do!" The Trump laughed in his throat, and he cancelled the aid that keeps families afloat. And he chuckled, and clucked, "What a great Trumpy trick! Without SNAP or insurance, the suffering starts quick!” Then he slithered and slunk, with a smile most unpleasant, raising prices with tariffs on every present. Oh, the billionaires with their tax cuts, they laughed — campaign bribes always work on a man who's so daft. Now, the problem today is that unlike the Grinch, the Trump will not change. His heart won't grow one inch. The Whos need us to stop him and his Trumpy friends. Fighting back is the way that the suffering ends. As we enter the new year, we know what to do: We'll make our country work for the many, not the few. Your support means the world as we fight to be free. But for now, Merry Christmas! — The WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Working Families Party and Working Families Power, please .

subject: Boebert just spit on every Colorado Republican

| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001R6ovUDR2xGUUohFo3vE1NUmSLvTrgF7ywor5Qlvf3xqnlnlmVJTfRO9QcctvzSubFAjyY7KOUiPF-4a_SzJufYro_2tQyQ11tjHXi8YWr-yzOqMB8MjhllrxT67Lr0NtnE-IREnGMUaq40PaA9ZqBdsgHqTp2nFjks4ThVDN8Mll7TQHjfyeQfQEYQFudKBJNdkHMG9bfCbuLv0_15hFia1GKJWF4xgE&c=rPoAz_UGC0Cm5egGceNLeyJyOfF4byX9H4gUl5pHoMHbaNbMjd2Q7g==&ch=Jr-gBg_Y0P_AjQrFGl_j1xY33FPJQjCCI_pjqOMuvY5RxxMvPI2tLQ==)

Peter,
In a move of pure political vengeance, the Trump administration has rejected disaster relief for Coloradans affected by fires and flooding.
It is retribution for the state's refusal to release Tina Peters, a former Mesa Colorado county clerk serving a nine year state sentence for letting unauthorized operatives access voting machines in an attempt to prop up Trump's 2020 election lie.
And here's the kicker: The people in desperate need of these funds are all from deep-red counties.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001R6ovUDR2xGUUohFo3vE1NUmSLvTrgF7ywor5Qlvf3xqnlnlmVJTfRO9QcctvzSubFAjyY7KOUiPF-4a_SzJufYro_2tQyQ11tjHXi8YWr-yzOqMB8MjhllrxT67Lr0NtnE-IREnGMUaq40PaA9ZqBdsgHqTp2nFjks4ThVDN8Mll7TQHjfyeQfQEYQFudKBJNdkHMG9bfCbuLv0_15hFiQTKbMJriBH1&c=rPoAz_UGC0Cm5egGceNLeyJyOfF4byX9H4gUl5pHoMHbaNbMjd2Q7g==&ch=Jr-gBg_Y0P_AjQrFGl_j1xY33FPJQjCCI_pjqOMuvY5RxxMvPI2tLQ==)
---
Lauren Boebert just spit on every Republican in Colorado. This will especially hurt constituents in her old and new congressional districts since both have been ravaged by floods and fires.
Boebert has spent years as one of Tina Peter's loudest cheerleaders, fueling the fire that brought us to this moment. Now, the chicken has come home to roost. Her extremism has invited this chaos and cruelty into our backyards, and it's Coloradans who are paying the price.
It's time to end Boebert's cruel circus act. Democrat and former science & tech civil servant Trisha Calvarese is running to replace Lauren Boebert and restore representation to this district.
Boebert is more vulnerable than ever. Boebert is only in Congress because of a razor-thin margin of just 546 votes. Last time Trisha ran, she delivered the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, outperforming Kamala Harris by 6 points.
[We have the momentum and the right candidate. We just need you on our side: Will you rush a contribution of $25, $15, $5 or whatever you can spare to help us defeat Lauren Boebert and stop this chaos?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001R6ovUDR2xGUUohFo3vE1NUmSLvTrgF7ywor5Qlvf3xqnlnlmVJTfRO9QcctvzSub0GsqSxJOTCNSTyad2Zu9Njwy2-6nSj-rWB-9yXbaw9ez6ORlzjPmxWZ0loG3BjWX8g_JgembPY0Lhs6ikWGQY0mrdKvXdjxYxZKNB8LHd6G67e1oBQhk54aEZkNZClImY7Eo2As8BjY00wCjas72-B_rVR-vLNOlthp7E_HCG0I=&c=rPoAz_UGC0Cm5egGceNLeyJyOfF4byX9H4gUl5pHoMHbaNbMjd2Q7g==&ch=Jr-gBg_Y0P_AjQrFGl_j1xY33FPJQjCCI_pjqOMuvY5RxxMvPI2tLQ==)

name: Deb Haaland
subject: When I think about the holiday season…
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
Every family deserves to have a fair shot.

| | |
---
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1102?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=1&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
Hey friend — it's Deb. When I think about the holiday season, I think about the parents who are putting on a brave face for their children while they try to make ends meet. I know that feeling well.
About fifteen years ago, my child and I were struggling. The only food in our cupboard was pinto beans and flour, and I wanted to give them a real holiday meal. I applied for emergency food stamps, but I was denied. I broke down in tears because I didn’t know how to tell my child: _it’s expensive to be poor._
That night, we ate what we had, and I tried to keep their spirits up. **This year, many families are doing the same thing, trying to stay strong for their kids even when things feel uncertain. It shouldn’t be this hard.**
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2932?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=2&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
Friend, these moments shaped who I am. It’s why I strongly believe leaders should make people’s lives easier, not harder. We all want good roads, safe communities, strong education, access to parks, and affordable, healthy food to eat.
**_[I want every family in New Mexico to have a fair shot. That's why I'm running to be the next governor of this beautiful state that I'm proud to call home.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2932?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=3&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)_**
| [Donate >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2932?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=4&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
---
I know the fights we face today are bigger than ever — and I’m prepared to get to work _immediately_. On day one, I will begin tackling the rising affordability crisis and healthcare costs, so we can build a state where every single person is safe, healthy, and can thrive. _But I can’t do this work alone._
Our grassroots-powered campaign was built for the people, by the people — and that includes you, friend. **_[So please, can I count on you to chip in $5, $10, or whatever you can comfortably spare, to help fuel our grassroots movement, win in New Mexico, and build a better future for working families?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2932?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=5&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)_**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $10 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2933?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=6&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
---
| | [Donate $35 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2934?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=7&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
---
| | [Donate $50 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2935?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=8&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
---
| | [Other Amount >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2932?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=9&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
---
Be fierce,

Deb

|
* * *
| | [Donate >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2932?refcode=jtk104-fr-nd&t=10&akid=6409%2E400544%2EG3qoMo)
---

disclaimer_text: paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign
body:
Election Day is just ten weeks away, and early voting begins in eight weeks. What we raise right now will determine our ability to reach voters | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/last-night-14191778?e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | [ ](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=d665c3d3b1&e=5d8d80652b)

| | , Merry Christmas and happy holidays to you and your loved ones. Our next and most important fundraising deadline is **December 31, just eight days away**. [**As we head into the final stretch before the primary, I’m asking you to give again today if you are able.**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=e487f1d2aa&e=5d8d80652b) **Election Day is just ten weeks away** , and **early voting begins in eight weeks**. What we raise right now will determine our ability to put yard signs up across Texas, reach voters by mail and text, and make the calls needed to turn people out to vote. This is the moment when preparation becomes victory. I have your back. And right now, I need you to have mine. Onward!

name: Benson HQ
subject: These are the stakes
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Can you give us a moment of your time?
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=1176%2E181023%2EB_7IMK)
Hi team,
We have a _major_ fundraising deadline ahead of us, and we need your help. This is our last fundraising deadline before the election year.
This is huge because we need to head into 2026 in a strong position to win. We need to prove to everyone that Jocelyn’s campaign is ready for whatever our opponents throw at us. Because the stakes in 2026 are high — healthcare costs, affordable housing, and Michigan’s education are all on the ballot — and we’re here to win.
**[Can you pitch in $10 right now to help us crush our goal?](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251223_AM1_V1_S1&amount=10&t=2&akid=1176%2E181023%2EB_7IMK)**
[DONATE $10](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251223_AM1_V1_S1&amount=10&t=3&akid=1176%2E181023%2EB_7IMK)
---
We’re neck-and-neck with Jocelyn’s MAGA opponent in the polls, and his campaign is pulling out all the stops with wealthy, deep-pocketed special interests to get ahead. He’s already received a $5 million check from the DeVos family.
And John James’ campaign outraised ours last time — which means we need to get ahead.
We don’t send these pesky emails to clog up your inbox! We would not be sending you this message right now if we didn’t genuinely believe that your donation would make a difference in helping us keep Michigan out of the hands of a governor who would amplify Trump’s agenda. _Those are the stakes._
**[So in the last week of 2025, ahead of our deadline, we’re humbly asking: Can you chip in $10 today to help me fight for Michigan?](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251223_AM1_V1_S1&amount=10&t=4&akid=1176%2E181023%2EB_7IMK)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/604?c0=jmb-em-q42025.ecTtCp&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251223_AM1_V1_S1&email=&amount=10&t=5&akid=1176%2E181023%2EB_7IMK)

---

body:
Minnesota Republicans want to bring Donald Trump’s MAGA agenda to Minnesota.
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251223-339&t=1&akid=339%2E34582%2ErXehxn)
Peter: we wanted you to see the Star Tribune’s latest reporting —
[](https://act.timwalz.com/go/217?t=2&akid=339%2E34582%2ErXehxn)
**“Nearly every candidate ... said they are seeking Trump’s endorsement in the race.”**
The Republicans running for Governor have made it clear — they want to bring Donald Trump’s MAGA agenda to Minnesota.
_**We can’t let that happen.**_
The GOP field wants to roll back the progress we’ve made together. They want to take away free school lunches for children, they want to ban abortion, they want to take away paid family and medical leave — and they want all this just to appease Donald Trump and their ultra-wealthy friends.
**[We’re doing everything we can to keep this from happening, but we need your help to keep Donald Trump’s MAGA agenda out of Minnesota. Can you rush $5 to make sure we’re ready to fight back?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251223.8h-buc&amount=5&refcode=em20251223-339&t=3&akid=339%2E34582%2ErXehxn)**

name: PADORA HQ - HAPPY HOLIDAYS!
subject: Wishing you peace, rest, and hope this holiday season
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Padora for Colorado
body:
A message of gratitude, reflection, and hope as we close out a hard year together.

| [|
---](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/3-ex7phtnFGA-_bUxLuVzg~~/AAUS1hA~/SoFllpGyPnGL7RlsefI0Udd0uHnSBEmZGag7S0nx7p0ZCgkW-mXDnhQKl-FicyBES8A7LsHkbRaty20T_ZYdi1Oz1A30_AfMgkp1WJS-BQJ3KEku4lk4Fwzdg4sfwG4u)
| | Friend, As this year comes to a close, I wanted to pause and reach out with a simple message from my family to yours: **Merry Christmas, Happy Holidays, and thank you.** And I want to say that to **all of our neighbors** , to our **Christian families** , to our **Muslim neighbors** , to our **Sikh neighbors** , to our **Jewish neighbors** , and to **everyone who practices faith, tradition, or reflection in their own way** during this season. This time of year means many things to many people, but at its heart, it is about **community, compassion, generosity, and hope**. Those values cross every faith and every background, and they bind us together far more than what divides us. This has been a **heavy year** for a lot of us. For so many families, the holidays don’t feel carefree right now. **Groceries cost more. Rent and mortgages are higher. Healthcare bills linger.** And for working parents, the pressure never really lets up, even during the season that’s supposed to bring rest. **I hear that everywhere I go.** And this year, I’ve gone just about everywhere in **Colorado’s Fourth District**. Since launching this campaign, we’ve held **town halls, community conversations, kitchen-table meetings** , and long drives between small towns, booming suburbs, sprawling cities and our rural communities. I’ve listened to **thousands of voters** share what keeps them up at night, and what they hope their kids might inherit one day. What I’ve heard is consistent: **People want dignity.**
**They want honesty.**
**They want leaders who listen more than they talk.**
**And they want a country that works for people who show up and do their part.** Together, **we’ve built something real**. Not a consultant-driven operation.
Not a campaign fueled by backroom donor lists.
But a **true grassroots movement** , grounded in conversations, relationships, and trust. We didn’t spend this year **chained to phones begging for money**.
We spent it **traveling the district, learning, showing up, and earning support face to face**.
And that work is already **making waves**. That work was only possible because of **YOU!** And despite all the negativity we see in the world, despite the noise and division that dominate headlines, I want you to know this: **I see kindness everywhere.** I see neighbors helping neighbors.
I see people listening with empathy.
I see the opportunity for **peace, personal growth, and understanding** if we are willing to choose it. I believe deeply that we can build a country that **lives in harmony rather than division** , that respects differences without fear, and that finds strength in our shared humanity. As we head into the new year, I want you to know how grateful I am for **every person who has supported this campaign** , whether by showing up, spreading the word, sending encouragement, or contributing when they could. If you’re in a position to do so, even **chipping in $5** before the end of the year would help us **get over the line and start 2026 on the right foot**. But please know this, **your support is not measured in dollars alone**. It’s measured in belief, in time, and in shared purpose. This campaign exists because people like you believe **Colorado deserves better** , and that **working-class voices belong in Congress**. From my family to yours, and to **every family and faith across our district** , **Happy Holidays**. I’m looking ahead to the new year with **hope, resolve, and deep gratitude**. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | [Chip in $10 now >>](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/Wvzdk5ZYzJDtCgdzpRFJDA~~/AAUS1hA~/1ydvEj_nKxjyvfYeKaIzrx-8bTZRjrwSj_vEaQCmLaVXAPO9x4mo6maj4jDlekKbrhjIs_2fEapyOJCA4m6EeZVUe5vIpT803UNNyC2NG4bx92ECm07PV2uy7EDo5UW70SrU80e2ZZ4F-mjtpkjQWvuKSjy-21LfKJOIMOXewEAB70ePcZ1blwzEvT0EekSo) | [Chip in $25 now >>](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/o1G1azZCnTy_Dc_1SRbDlg~~/AAUS1hA~/1ydvEj_nKxjyvfYeKaIzrx-8bTZRjrwSj_vEaQCmLaVXAPO9x4mo6maj4jDlekKbrhjIs_2fEapyOJCA4m6EeZVUe5vIpT803UNNyC2NG4Y8NVfmCkSkQYBDLr-YJSPFXdMnItRYa5PmQnTAQfg7NzkQgAJ3Jqjqos8-pqBA1bRp0Yms-JhLT_7ZtGarqcrO)

**Let us choose empathy, extend grace to one another, and carry peace and love with us into the days ahead.**
**John Padora**
Working Dad • Fighter • Engineer
Candidate for Congress in CO 04

name: =?UTF-8?Q?Eduardo_Ver=C3=A1stegui?=
subject: Friend, your love can bring a child home for Christmas
body:
A child should fall asleep on Christmas Eve listening for reindeer hooves...

| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e17289370/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvakhjM3l2NkRrag/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsbDRHRmRNRQ/qP2hpZHM9bmN2UjRTZW0zdUJRejBscw/gaUrXGg/sbr369d35ab)
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Friend, A child should fall asleep on Christmas Eve listening for reindeer hooves... **Not to footsteps in the hallway outside a locked room.** **Right now through December 31, your gift to the Sound of Freedom Foundation will be MATCHED—dollar for dollar—so you can do DOUBLE the good for God’s children.**
---
* **$25 = $50**
* **$50 = $100**
* **$100 = $200**

---
But this opportunity ends **at midnight on December 31.**
---
[ **Double My Gift Before December 31** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
My name is **Eduardo Verástegui**. I produced the film _Sound of Freedom_ , and I now serve as President of the **Sound of Freedom Foundation**. I want you to know: **I've given my life to fight for God’s children who are trapped in the nightmare of sex trafficking.** Right now, while families are hanging lights and wrapping presents, there are children who don’t even know what day it is. They are far from home. No tree. No lights. No family. Only the constant terror of being used, bought, and sold by adults who see them as a product — not as sons and daughters. Some parents are staring at an empty seat at the table this Christmas.
A stocking that will stay limp.
A bed that’s made... and cold. That is the brutal reality of child sex trafficking. And this Christmas, I’m asking you to do something beautiful and bold: **help bring a child home for Christmas.**__
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
If you watched _Sound of Freedom_ and thought, “I wish there were something real I could do,” this is that moment. Because a movie is not enough. We needed a mission.
---
We launched the Sound of Freedom Foundation to:
* partner with trusted investigators, law enforcement, and aftercare teams to help **locate and rescue children

**
* help build the evidence needed to **arrest and prosecute traffickers

**
* support survivors with **safe shelter and trauma-informed care** so they can begin to heal.

---
All of this flows from one unshakable conviction... **God’s children are NOT for sale.** And here is something I need you to understand: we cannot do any of this without people like you. Every investigation we help fuel...
every safe bed prepared for the moment a child walks free...
every trauma counselor who sits with a survivor as the nightmares spill out...
every step toward healing and restoration... All of it depends on someone deciding: **“Not on my watch. Not this Christmas.”** That’s you. When you give, you step into the story as the hero. We are simply the guide—connecting your courage and generosity to children who need it most. And right now, your compassion goes twice as far. [**Give before December 31 and your gift is matched dollar for dollar.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ Double My Gift: Bring a Child Home ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
I want you to picture a moment with me. A child has been rescued. For the first time in a long time, they sleep in a safe bed. They wake up to soft light instead of harsh neon.
Someone tells them, **“You’re safe now. No one here will hurt you.”** Behind the scenes, a team is working—confirming identities, contacting authorities, and searching for family members who have been praying, weeping, and waiting. Sometimes it takes months.
Sometimes years. Then one day, in an airport or at a border crossing, a door opens... a mother sees the child she thought was gone forever... there are no speeches, no cameras—just tears, shaking shoulders, and a whispered, **“Thank You, God.”** **This is the kind of miracle your generosity can help make possible.** Not every reunion happens exactly on December 25th. But the heart of Christmas—**God stepping into our darkness and bringing His children home—is what drives everything we do.** Traffickers don’t take holidays off.
So we can’t either. That’s why I’m asking you—urgently and personally—to make your year-end gift **before midnight on December 31** , while it will still be doubled.
---
[ Yes, Double My Gift Today ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Because somewhere, right now, there is a child who feels utterly forgotten. Your gift is a message that whispers into that darkness: **“I see you. I’m fighting for you. I’m helping bring you home.”** **[Will you help bring a child home for Christmas?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)**
---
With gratitude, Eduardo Verástegui
Sound of Freedom, Producer
For the Sound of Freedom Foundation
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
P.S. The match ends**December 31 at midnight. Please give now so your gift will be doubled.**
---
[ Support the Sound of Freedom Foundation ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
6671 Southwest Freeway, Suite 700, Houston TX 77074
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e17289370/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsbDRHRmRNRQ/qP2hpZHM9bmN2UjRTZW0zdUJRejBscw/gaUrXGg/sbp5fddad78)

name: Kevin Kijewski for Michigan Attorney General
subject: 🇺🇸🎄Merry Christmas from Kevin Kijewski for Michigan Attorney General 🇺🇸🎄
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kevin Kijewski for Michigan, 115 W Allegan St, Ste 900, Lansing, MI 48933_
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/kevinformi/merry-christmas-from-kevin-kijewski-for-michigan-attorney-general-4741186?e=4b10910eed)
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=eaf1812c74&e=4b10910eed)
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=0333ce304d&e=4b10910eed)
| | | | | |
# Merry Christmas Michigan!
---
| | Dear Friend and Patriot,
As we gather with loved ones to celebrate the birth of our Savior, I am reminded of the profound promise in Isaiah 7:14: "Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel." This prophecy, fulfilled in the humble manger in Bethlehem, brings us hope, peace, and the assurance that God is with always with us – especially in these challenging times for our families here in Michigan.
This season of faith and family has been especially joyful for us. My wife, our daughter, her grandparents, and I recently took her to visit Santa at the Finnish Center Association in Farmington Hills – her wide-eyed wonder and excitement captured the pure magic of Christmas.

Last night, I had the privilege of [attending the Annual Michigan College Republicans Gala](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=3dd4a3b5fb&e=4b10910eed) at the beautiful Grosse Pointe War Memorial. It was inspiring to connect with passionate young conservatives from chapters across our state, celebrating Christ’s birth while building the next generation of leaders committed to freedom, opportunity, and Michigan values. I’m also happy to share our campaign has been **endorsed by Tyler Campbell, Chairman of the Michigan College Republicans**. Tyler and his dedicated team are the future – and with their energy, we'll make Michigan red again in 2026!

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | | Merry Christmas Michigan! | | Dear Friend and Patriot, As we gather with loved ones to celebrate the birth of our Savior, I am reminded of the profound promise in Isaiah 7:14: "Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel." This prophecy, fulfilled in the humble manger in Bethlehem, brings us hope, peace, and the assurance that God is with always with us – especially in these challenging times for our families here in Michigan. This season of faith and family has been especially joyful for us. My wife, our daughter, her grandparents, and I recently took her to visit Santa at the Finnish Center Association in Farmington Hills – her wide-eyed wonder and excitement captured the pure magic of Christmas. Last night, I had the privilege of at the beautiful Grosse Pointe War Memorial. It was inspiring to connect with passionate young conservatives from chapters across our state, celebrating Christ’s birth while building the next generation of leaders committed to freedom, opportunity, and Michigan values. I’m also happy to share our campaign has been endorsed by Tyler Campbell, Chairman of the Michigan College Republicans. Tyler and his dedicated team are the future – and with their energy, we'll make Michigan red again in 2026! | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | The Year Ahead: Your support as grassroots leaders and delegates is invaluable in building the momentum to replace the current radical leadership with strong, conservative principles. As we close out 2025 and approach the March endorsement convention, . Every contribution fuels town halls, outreach, and the fight to make Michigan safe, prosperous, and red again. | | Merry Christmas and Let’s Keep Winning! From my family to yours, may your Christmas be filled with joy, peace, and the light of Christ. God bless you and your family. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family.
