He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. But first, I want to talk about why I’m supporting Katie, and why your support matters.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251223_2_ak.jL_hA9&refcode=em20251223-40607&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40607%2E3422442%2EDNBv8c)**
Katie shares the lived experience of millions of Californians she’s running to represent — people who work hard, who want to raise healthy kids in good homes without it costing an arm and a leg, and who understand what it’s like to anxiously review their receipts after visiting the grocery store.
**Katie has what it takes to lead her state through this affordability crisis.**
But a statewide election in California requires a lot of money, and Katie can’t bankroll her own race with her billions of dollars. And she’s not taking contributions from corporations and lobbyists.
**[She’s counting on grassroots supporters to power her campaign and win in California. So, can you make a donation of $5 or whatever makes sense for you to support Katie’s campaign?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251223_2_ak.jL_hA9&refcode=em20251223-40607&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40607%2E3422442%2EDNBv8c)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251223_2_ak.jL_hA9&refcode=em20251223-40607&t=23&akid=40607%2E3422442%2EDNBv8c)
I still remember the day when I met Katie. My consumer law class was early in the morning, meaning a lot of students had their heads down, trying to avoid being called on.
But not Katie. She was determined and ready to go. When the first answer she gave wasn’t her strongest, she came to my office hours and said, “Please keep calling on me. I am going to learn to do this.”
Getting to know her, I saw her bone-deep commitment to making a difference in people’s lives.
When Katie decided to run for Congress in 2018, I was very happy to support her. I knew that we needed more people like her in Washington — who understood the real struggles that working families faced, who had the courage to stand up to big corporations and special interests, and who would fight to make our government work for everyone.
A Democrat hadn’t represented her district for decades, but she knew exactly how to personally connect with voters, build a strong movement, and win. She flipped that seat, and she’s been winning for working people ever since.
Simply put, Katie delivers. She’s smart and she isn’t afraid. She stands up to Wall Street and Big Pharma, she holds fossil fuel companies accountable, and she fights to protect Californians’ rights against Trump’s far-right administration. She is exactly who Californians need as their next governor.
**[So please, if you can, make a donation of $5 or whatever makes sense to Katie’s campaign. We’re going to keep fighting to make our government work for working people — and we need you in this fight.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251223_2_ak.jL_hA9&refcode=em20251223-40607&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=24&akid=40607%2E3422442%2EDNBv8c)**
Thanks for being a part of this,
Elizabeth

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251223-40607&amounts=&t=25&refcode2=40607_3422442_DNBv8c&akid=40607%2E3422442%2EDNBv8c)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=40607&t=26&akid=40607%2E3422442%2EDNBv8c)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=40607)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=40607&t=27&akid=40607%2E3422442%2EDNBv8c)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026

name: Adam Steen
subject: From the pitcher's mound to the governor's mansion
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Adam Steen for Iowa
body:
I grew up in Indianola, where I learned the importance of faith, hard work, and service. Now I am ready to serve the people of Iowa. |
---

[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6dea61476e1949fcb7f78f6eef2aef84&_e=YV2vLKdHySpgAHjiwKHPivMoooZndKk8GP__C03FiJFszvPjRBjQbK41CwX-P_Csy_g_ZuOeO0dWW6LlrOEnc-Ak3Qvy6cz6qeBBrGyVWhW3oQc1G6Uw_MdPninX6tzrlnljc1fCVRB6j1w7OXeR2OP3i38XQktjsOoVjiha8AyFXZYLpNJcC_kuHN1Zs8FoaaFLNoKlyplB5dl_w878C4Rzxip3XSXX0A0zrVmvqgqXG3srrNlCwD_dmdGouBz67Ji_BVyi5k8NTPlPb6DJ2d5e1ZeDcWRqDDOKDRDBdH-JLoeEONgEbtMTEgDjmC31grXSq6Ea4a0GalmN-Lb-niKCcQJpQI1ydPreiZUy85QS9lRFsWgkU0dH0IXbHsWBmmjFO9zuqkq6ak-6oHt3O0xIVm0dkaL1EZCp-I0aW-Yxx-pBQDJTJnPCWGJ7tRtlwrldf9AAID2vPSXtCORbQlC8r---llIKCux_Ec-DXcrdOfe-Rzx8mZInp94irOf8HXqz2cHTjdFMOp16YKSKSPS-y06oM8YDjQVpnUMfex2cHphvDTvCAUASPf04EaAXSL8ioptSiuCj3qNzOdJd7rbrsuS3UWseqp69K7vEc43UyZZaqf9mjtjh3c8yC2UlsHTjXMunuBoruRWEIeAwAQ8cI0gDsJORM9sH0xXOHs4ziCp_liuOcR-AyPMjHbtRgwOEzZhT1pWtnk287Z4FSeLApNWSuhlxiiAWpwzWysM%3D)

Fellow American - let me introduce myself.

My name is Adam Steen, and I am running to be the next governor of Iowa.

I want you to know my story, and if it resonates with you, I would appreciate your support today.

I grew up in Indianola, Iowa, where my parents taught me the importance of faith, hard work, and serving others. After graduating, I played college baseball on a scholarship and was drafted by the Philadelphia Phillies.

[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6dea61476e1949fcb7f78f6eef2aef84&_e=YV2vLKdHySpgAHjiwKHPivMoooZndKk8GP__C03FiJFszvPjRBjQbK41CwX-P_Csy_g_ZuOeO0dWW6LlrOEnc-Ak3Qvy6cz6qeBBrGyVWhW3oQc1G6Uw_MdPninX6tzrlnljc1fCVRB6j1w7OXeR2OP3i38XQktjsOoVjiha8AyFXZYLpNJcC_kuHN1Zs8FoaaFLNoKlyplB5dl_w878C4Rzxip3XSXX0A0zrVmvqgqXG3srrNlCwD_dmdGouBz6E3splyask-026TsZFuinrVTRVWFRhmHtTlnZBw8aOZP4wceWIgaXHSd6aarMQHct-vs45_Sgg1pI-NYE4s_EN-6POqPCBiO9xNVAZTZD_JJKjxURmOAqVgvGbONAH4oVUZnMzvD6pv3c8BNGvySPadhk-1-ikoctZ1CT0lR8yir45I1XkYgT0gLpNLVcxeIHqfWj_ejkkK_wFBwS4besDRGnXBoDBO3sINuiplWXlDG0WkmMQBKqfhO8r6YbIne7-WcDPzL-g3ssi-dTuRJ3QMNRTuiQb-uiz1FH5-6lUhLDk-XbmJqpCAaXZGgR-3Cuk0w33DHLeEYS3pfiIwshbh1TPtGmE_qYwmYOHTuL67m5eJjx0Fb_WCE6uGWzfKnEtbX-LvXknTHz9b0idS9UUs7Cwb0aYWAPG7qUdK1rtU1S6giqYlSUbI9sJuFV-DTECoYRTOVhA-Huh1zi94tRqekcENOuYI85SKKM0ekOFXQ%3D)
When my baseball career ended, I knew it was time to return home to Iowa and build my own small business, as well as start a family. My business leadership led to my recruitment to work for the Governor's office, where I reduced waste, streamlined processes, and delivered projects on time while never losing sight of the government's responsibility to serve the taxpayer.

**Fellow American, I want to do the same thing as the Governor of Iowa.**

Our state is stronger than ever, but that doesn't mean we can sit back and relax. We must continue to engage, work, and fight.

But I cannot do this alone. I need pro-family conservatives from here in Iowa and across the country to step up. **[Can I count on you to join our team by adding your endorsement?](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6dea61476e1949fcb7f78f6eef2aef84&_e=YV2vLKdHySpgAHjiwKHPivMoooZndKk8GP__C03FiJFszvPjRBjQbK41CwX-P_Csy_g_ZuOeO0dWW6LlrOEnc-Ak3Qvy6cz6qeBBrGyVWhW3oQc1G6Uw_MdPninX6tzrlnljc1fCVRB6j1w7OXeR2OP3i38XQktjsOoVjiha8AyFXZYLpNJcC_kuHN1Zs8FoaaFLNoKlyplB5dl_w878C4Rzxip3XSXX0A0zrVmvqgqXG3srrNlCwD_dmdGouBz6AnOXAm36UJw2A4VfNDtral0oyUrE9WXzr9cET7f6X_TlupDnFmLBnoTFP1XeMkfM-Flhaz6YfuLKjdaX9yXHqHfWjn_7is-8nuF2OV7yKv220oZToayoPQO2dwVDs-YpBjadCkPIYX4_sxJoDRd9Qie1k_YZrAYS2he-4ypEYuAynpcjGENnSpPr04hTF177OOW2qMjPdd-RAUB_mH3124F3H2v1f0_benrJ4_qTDvr0It35XeGg6iQv5qHNyaxaBW5LV3VlNnBb1XCJN152DydGVS4epKfhbD5aY760pYRVxpiDxbzlsoHUWpXNgqjTvYxV_J_FBFrDbflQwESCHImeN_I06HI0E_QxM7GhazQlIAiezLzRlpdDZjXTT7dt2xPFWqYmYTBL5mQtrVUcyIWeBfJdCKFC_x4ibvXZz959u4PY7Blkn-SaEZFcUEwg0m7ZHlI0JoDA7Zbkf_wKInaIqVXHAnPpYPswsR4VP8U%3D)**

[**ENDORSE ADAM FOR GOVERNOR →**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6dea61476e1949fcb7f78f6eef2aef84&_e=YV2vLKdHySpgAHjiwKHPivMoooZndKk8GP__C03FiJFszvPjRBjQbK41CwX-P_Csy_g_ZuOeO0dWW6LlrOEnc-Ak3Qvy6cz6qeBBrGyVWhW3oQc1G6Uw_MdPninX6tzrlnljc1fCVRB6j1w7OXeR2OP3i38XQktjsOoVjiha8AyFXZYLpNJcC_kuHN1Zs8FoaaFLNoKlyplB5dl_w878C4Rzxip3XSXX0A0zrVmvqgqXG3srrNlCwD_dmdGouBz6IJSC2AjOJnvs2mL1NNOrox9OCUmHFvT13_llOR2_bykA7o7xUCP4OzZdaYZoQYAv2diq7ZSm8bTC_mFEDx8Jrpiwx_-UW4kT8kTV7wV4WlynpK98ILPUlnxdp1iMcJm1hEfXdaMJAJOl0VRKPu4Ws2npZQNSDJD5WxsJe9dASSZPQpF5C2mk5NEAvLO7pB0Q2Ix_Se-kDe99NS5ag5ouHIT4BMrKhlHL4kZfjVLzltSWnpdt8wZbEs4X81SM47urJSVu85M6YnPUsAQkCnVQeWHk-bJ5-jJDOKnrBxWldAae7n2hHX-4zHng9P9ZjZGa3BoOQvzAkQjEEEFFh8904N99UEmpxtzplUH1dgtPrLfiB8SuTXE1otMZ38xHbijo-1rGZ8nx0jC8ZCEAS0Fph-1agfOFwlFNPnRbK6GTFdzWJNld0FZ4jYpc-yB-bZAzwCKcqtFEaWif8K20t3-oiiIlDT1-8CW3sHL-xMYS5o4%3D)
I am not a career politician. I did not spend years in Washington, D.C, perfecting the art of all talk and no action. That's why I need people like you to build the momentum of our campaign.

I know this is a longer email, but if you'd made it all the way to the end, you must have read something you liked, Fellow American.

clean_body: I grew up in Indianola, where I learned the importance of faith, hard work, and service. Now I am ready to serve the people of Iowa. | Fellow American - let me introduce myself. My name is Adam Steen, and I am running to be the next governor of Iowa. I want you to know my story, and if it resonates with you, I would appreciate your support today. I grew up in Indianola, Iowa, where my parents taught me the importance of faith, hard work, and serving others. After graduating, I played college baseball on a scholarship and was drafted by the Philadelphia Phillies. When my baseball career ended, I knew it was time to return home to Iowa and build my own small business, as well as start a family. My business leadership led to my recruitment to work for the Governor's office, where I reduced waste, streamlined processes, and delivered projects on time while never losing sight of the government's responsibility to serve the taxpayer. Fellow American, I want to do the same thing as the Governor of Iowa. Our state is stronger than ever, but that doesn't mean we can sit back and relax. We must continue to engage, work, and fight. But I cannot do this alone. I need pro-family conservatives from here in Iowa and across the country to step up. I am not a career politician. I did not spend years in Washington, D.C, perfecting the art of all talk and no action. That's why I need people like you to build the momentum of our campaign. I know this is a longer email, but if you'd made it all the way to the end, you must have read something you liked, Fellow American. Thanks, Adam Steen Conservative for Iowa | | Paid for by Adam Steen for Iowa Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

| | Dear Fellow American,

It's hard to believe that we are just weeks away from 2026, the year that will mark 250 years since the founding of our nation.

This remarkable milestone is a great opportunity to brush up on your knowledge of our Founding Fathers and the heroic work they did to create America.

**That 's why I'd like to offer you a free digital copy of The Heritage Foundation's book, _American Founders: Leaders at the Creation of the Republic._**

This comprehensive 300+ page guide brings to life 16 of the nation's most influential Founders--not as mythical figures, but as real statesmen confronting the immense political challenges of their time.

From George Washington to Alexander Hamilton, you'll explore their pivotal decisions that laid the foundation for American liberty and self-government.

**You can get your free digital copy at the secure link below.**

[**https://secured.heritage.org/american-founders-ebook/**](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/ktw5QNgq77vpM0ms_bqVjD79YM40RW633xugOPH_HPtULghN8yiKrzFpnaoAsFzDbUjYTRGCxd_YZnBRdcXjPQTglAXGq_PAeWTi_6tDulbF4s_bagoAx_3wHTSr-iV-zQvZvnBkjzFdD28NIgkeNhOn5lXxM-YMwYsSV73P07NsAopj8MGvpPdyuFTIRZQzk4U-HQ84G5dhZObU4fwUh0aSSUW3rHhNj2Kwvv_DndcT_-o0j3XqkHDCWd1Oux8xX-kt-aiQId8oYkWB_D1mUPrEZIVgcJQnPxeYjrkp84-tfS4tApMW6pGhRg67tKtXR_XWVo-7i1iE7ILGcrvNYzX5C3jVMD6DRmPHxukeu9zyEDZG6ma2B-Yfk9vaGrivqH90s7G6r6HLZUVq41hXfCJyPPVeZ2RZ3fsgYXwS6bYdn2qoomgxPYshxG-oUpPWTyGGUhEpvNaC9lBEU13fFsD7D3tmwNzrZBCq2pcsckj_FDEaHQ2Eew6Z90yWy0mOLkqSvxaJFEKo6uw9Jtr8U9EzDr7QXWSi0AJudBn7c95IkibPJHJWrI4GHD3x2sPj_Un4rGjDKfGEtSw3jG1Nip11Ktra7i_0dCPPXoVGUgwuWn2yvXoLZrSJSd0xeSqYqq3DIJjLdwTB4L_W32sTsC1fOCWQVmyuDZGK5cFypVid/Fgj1pYbWK72qv0K-2LDpvYwefVVpvEXu/16)

I hope this proves to be a valuable resource as our nation prepares to celebrate our 250th birthday.

Best,

Tony Johnson
Director of Membership

**P.S. Sharpen your knowledge on America 's Founders before our nation's 250th birthday through this free eBook from The Heritage Foundation: [https://secured.heritage.org/american-founders-ebook/](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/8SB5Fn_jhZoOLl5vzS5sIxkKiHk7f7yVOXU46tYPk2vKfCHcrRf3ra26OPqOYRFe50cLW9lQusmtlQRdMaOgPK7fDDq-NVojEcUcSEKlEdyQu_OAt0T87GzkRzD4_qA-QYksmC1xt4XMYFBuQBGuzJKijM1efJ11QnXkSJZeKphNPLUX4oOTOT8Skc4st4KQUDlN3ohIvSv6BwdaiusVTnX5AXwJeQE7hXV9m0sc3G33HwH-xmMIfOnKd2is73mM_r4eBeD5YY61ghhYJk0IZvnqk8KWYz6Zbq6MReyA2OVT95OjJ7AGHvoPrXq9jEVXtTTxQuhqiPd3BfdWJB2OpAXeIQdEXfegxouVPamLx4AHDpkN2Eq1aTMmIC2g3wXTQd03zmLONom3fpGZebxYFLeVFuOdCFIdJp9Sv8zYnONU1pl85dnxQY0OPK5q64u6ay17mm1vkANyFiKR-F9Gt8S1nccFsCUc8CDqZihXRvO_1JZVoSzaxFgSgmxeJaFefwhm1gCAqRxRvg350volWchM1Z_KexJdUxd0OBcTmUCxuQ6KLXu2qWdWk6mexwy6pzBa9_kpiOHfrX1FuXkigM6ry8jyiwgNCampsvIP-86vRG80nZG1WKOhMjKf1NbmCQ-U1Vnv98iPg9oSioGY8tGVKgmJm5omX2pn_7fqkWo5/65ZzFvq2_EULBox_CZA3_ig1yadez-zo/16)**

That 's why I'd like to offer you a free digital copy of The Heritage Foundation's book, American Founders: Leaders at the Creation of the Republic. This comprehensive 300+ page guide brings to life 16 of the nation's most influential Founders--not as mythical figures, but as real statesmen confronting the immense political challenges of their time. From George Washington to Alexander Hamilton, you'll explore their pivotal decisions that laid the foundation for American liberty and self-government. You can get your free digital copy at the secure link below. I hope this proves to be a valuable resource as our nation prepares to celebrate our 250th birthday. Best, Tony Johnson Director of Membership P.S. Sharpen your knowledge on America 's Founders before our nation's 250th birthday through this free eBook from The Heritage Foundation: The Heritage Foundation 214 Massachusetts Avenue NE Washington D.C. 20002

name: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez
subject: Before you log off today
body:
| | Peter, I’ll be honest – asking for donations isn’t exactly my favorite thing to do. Especially right now, when so many families are already juggling tight budgets and the busy holiday season.

But, I wouldn’t ask if it didn’t matter. Next week’s FEC deadline is coming up fast, and what we report will set the tone for our ultra-competitive race heading into the on-year.

If you’re able, even a few dollars makes a real difference in showing we’re ready for the fight ahead:

name: Beto O'Rourke (via LeftNet)
subject: This is how we fight back – together
body:
Powered by People is turning it up in 2026. Add your name and be part of the movement.
#
Remember Trump’s inauguration? It wasn't even a year ago. All of the corporate titans and tech billionaires in supplication, pledging their fealty... soon to be followed by the major law firms, the Ivy League universities, the media corporations, even Congress and the Supreme Court... really, anyone with any kind of conventional power was bending the knee to this would-be dictator... it was enough to make you puke... and to tempt us to despair.
But instead, we chose to fight.
We held town halls in Republican strongholds, places Trump had just won with 70% of the vote. Amarillo, Wichita Falls, Tyler. We had them in the suburbs too: Mansfield, Rosenberg (with my friend Tim Walz), Denton, Garland, Humble. And we did the same in bigger cities, from San Antonio and Laredo to Beaumont.
That was just the beginning for us. We had a big year at Powered by People, and we’re not slowing down now. We’re only going to turn it up in 2026, because the stakes could not be higher and the opportunity could not be clearer.
This is how we win a U.S. Senate seat. This is how we win Democratic seats in Congress. This is how we flip state legislative seats, protect health care and voting rights, and make sure the people of Texas — not billionaires, not extremists, not corrupt politicians — decide our future.
And the way we do it is the same way we always have: together.
Powered by People exists for moments like this. Not just to protest what’s wrong, but to organize for what’s possible. To register voters, recruit and support candidates in every district, build coalitions across communities, and stay in the fight year-round — not just when there’s an election on the calendar.
If you’re ready to build on this work in 2026, I’m asking you to [**join us**](https://act.leftnet.org/go/22124?source=ads_ms_PBP_202512-EA-leftN-listr-2026join%3Fsource%3Dem20251223-1419&t=1&akid=1419%2E2118727%2E4c7tL7).
**[Sign up to be part of Powered by People and take your place in the largest grassroots organizing effort Texas has ever seen.](https://act.leftnet.org/go/22124?source=ads_ms_PBP_202512-EA-leftN-listr-2026join%3Fsource%3Dem20251223-1419&t=2&akid=1419%2E2118727%2E4c7tL7)** Whether you can volunteer, help register voters, show up at events, or simply stay connected and help spread the word, there is a role for you — and we need you in this fight.
**[ADD YOUR NAME ››](https://act.leftnet.org/go/22124?source=ads_ms_PBP_202512-EA-leftN-listr-2026join%3Fsource%3Dem20251223-1419&t=3&akid=1419%2E2118727%2E4c7tL7)**
Thanks for believing in the power of people. Thanks for showing up when it matters. And thanks for being in this with us — for Texas and for the country.
Beto
P.S. This movement is powered by people choosing action over despair. [**When you sign up, you’re joining a community committed to doing the hard work — every day — to protect our democracy and deliver a better future.**](https://act.leftnet.org/go/22124?source=ads_ms_PBP_202512-EA-leftN-listr-2026join+%3Fsource%3Dem20251223-1419&t=4&akid=1419%2E2118727%2E4c7tL7)

**LeftNet is an online political network fighting for progressive causes and candidates focused on social, racial, economic, and environmental justice.**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leftnet.org/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=5&akid=1419%2E2118727%2E4c7tL7)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leftnet.org/signup/update_your_information/?t=6&akid=1419%2E2118727%2E4c7tL7)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leftnet.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=1419%2E2118727%2E4c7tL7)

Email us: [team@leftnet.org](mailto:team@leftnet.org)

name: Christopher Ahuja for Congress
subject: In 10 Days, Premiums Spike. Can You Help Us Fight Back?
body:
We CAN demand better than this.

| | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=895cffc660&e=53c7d12fc7)
| | | | | | Dear Peter,
In just over a week, a health care crisis will hit our community.Families across California’s 32nd Congressional District are bracing for premiums to skyrocket. Some are preparing to cancel the plans they depend on because the costs are simply too high. And they’re making these decisions during the holidays.I’m hearing from people maxing out credit cards, borrowing from loved ones, or quietly deciding they may have to go without health care in 2026 just to keep a roof overhead and food on the table — hoping they don’t get sick.And while this crisis bears down on our community, Brad Sherman is nowhere to be found. Silent.After decades in Washington, he still doesn’t have the DNA to fight for people who are hurting. When I’m elected to Congress, I will make it my top priority to ensure people can afford the basics of a dignified life: health care, groceries, and a safe place to live. If you believe this economy is not affordable and that change is possible, this campaign is for you.[**Donate $25, $15, or even a few dollars today to fuel our outreach and rapid response in these critical days before costs spike.**](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=4e341b4636&e=53c7d12fc7) Together, we can demand better.
---
| | **Wishing you a peaceful holiday,**[](https://chrisahuja.us2.list-manage.com/track/click?u=67424df89870d1cac00e55f46&id=d896972f80&e=53c7d12fc7)**Chris Ahuja****Candidate for Congress, CA-32**
---

Peter,

**Before the year wraps up, I just want to say THANK YOU!**

You showed up, stood strong, and helped carry this team through a big season, and that means more than I can put into words.

As we head into Christmas, my hope is simple: slow down, hug your loved ones, and enjoy some well-earned time with family. That's what really matters.

**And if you 're blessed enough to give a little this season, consider supporting a charity that helps folks get back on their feet.**

_Proud to be on this team with you. Merry Christmas and God Bless._

_-Coach_

clean_body: Peter, I wanted to reach out to you personally one last time. The recent election losses hurt us all, and they showed exactly what happens when our Republican Party is outspent. Frankly, we cannot afford defeat in November. We need resources now to secure our ground game, sharpen our message, and fund our leadership to win big. I can only fight as hard as the resources allow me to. I cannot deliver the support the GOP needs in 2026 if we miss this opportunity to close the funding gap. And I'm turning to you for help because I've seen your supporter records: Your Elite status is still active. Your commitment as the only Platinum Member in your area is exactly the strategic advantage we need. * You are an essential bulwark for freedom, and you've earned the right to maximum influence. This isn't just a donation; it's an opportunity to maximize your impact on our movement. For every $1 you contribute, you'll be immediately matched by 4X. We know you may have missed this exclusive chance before, but it's not too late. This funding is critical to accelerating our path to a massive Republican victory in November. Seize this final chance to multiply your influence. am counting on you to step up now. Sincerely, Mike Pompeo 70th Secretary of State | | | | PAID FOR BY CHAMPION AMERICAN VALUES. body:
I’ve spent my career standing up to corruption, even when it’s politically risky. |
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121873469/590859453/313059105?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.23_OTS-Adam-Schiff_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGRiMTMyNC01M2UwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FoBjKUodDl9CVVzlURRLWVYchMlkQllytSL0sxq9z2k=) Team, this is Adam Schiff. I’ve spent my career standing up to corruption, even when it’s politically risky. I’ve prosecuted cases. I’ve led investigations and spoken hard truths when it mattered most. Like when I led Trump’s first impeachment. Or served on the January 6th Committee. And I have to be honest — this is one of the most challenging times of my entire career. Donald Trump is threatening our constitutional right to freedom of speech, undermining the rule of law, and chipping away at Americans’ trust in our institutions. Attacks are mounting against anyone who speaks out against this administration — whether you’re a current or former elected official, a journalist, a comedian or an ordinary citizen. And with Republicans in control of both the House and Senate, there are simply not enough checks and balances to stop the rampant corruption and lawlessness. So like I said, this is a very scary time — but not a time to back down. The GOP wants to overwhelm us and keep us from acting. But when we come together, we’re not powerless. We can organize, mobilize, and reclaim our country. **[_If you’re with me, make a contribution to my campaign and Off the Sidelines before our end-of-quarter fundraising deadline hits on December 31st. Together we can hit our fundraising goal, flip the Senate, and end this madness._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121873470/590859454/313059105?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.23_OTS-Adam-Schiff_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGRiMTMyNC01M2UwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FoBjKUodDl9CVVzlURRLWVYchMlkQllytSL0sxq9z2k=)** _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Adam Schiff and Off the Sidelines PAC will go through immediately:_ | | [ DONATE $10 >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121873471/590859455/313059105?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.23_OTS-Adam-Schiff_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGRiMTMyNC01M2UwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FoBjKUodDl9CVVzlURRLWVYchMlkQllytSL0sxq9z2k=)

clean_body: I’ve spent my career standing up to corruption, even when it’s politically risky. | = Team, this is Adam Schiff. I’ve spent my career standing up to corruption, even when it’s politically risky. I’ve prosecuted cases. I’ve led investigations and spoken hard truths when it mattered most. Like when I led Trump’s first impeachment. Or served on the January 6th Committee. And I have to be honest — this is one of the most challenging times of my entire career. Donald Trump is threatening our constitutional right to freedom of speech, undermining the rule of law, and chipping away at Americans’ trust in our institutions. Attacks are mounting against anyone who speaks out against this administration — whether you’re a current or former elected official, a journalist, a comedian or an ordinary citizen. And with Republicans in control of both the House and Senate, there are simply not enough checks and balances to stop the rampant corruption and lawlessness. So like I said, this is a very scary time — but not a time to back down. The GOP wants to overwhelm us and keep us from acting. But when we come together, we’re not powerless. We can organize, mobilize, and reclaim our country. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Adam Schiff and Off the Sidelines PAC will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = Thanks for stepping up, Adam © 2025 | Paid for by Off the Sidelines PAC

name: Brad Finstad
subject: Over $9 Billion Lost. Enough Is Enough.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Finstad for Congress
body:
| | | | |
---
| Friends, This week, federal prosecutors confirmed what Minnesotans have feared for years:
**Half or more of the $18 BILLION billed through Minnesota state programs since 2018 may be fraudulent.**
That’s **over $9 billion** stolen from taxpayers.
Let that sink in.
According to the U.S. Attorney’s Office, this fraud spans at least 14 separate state programs, including Medicaid, housing assistance, autism services, and disability care. Criminals literally traveled from out of state because, in their own words, _Minnesota was “a good opportunity to make money.”_
That doesn’t happen by accident. It happens when one-party control, weak oversight, and media silence create the perfect environment for abuse.
I’ve been fighting this corruption head-on.
I’ve introduced legislation to force transparency, require real data-sharing, and finally give federal watchdogs the tools they need to shut fraud down before billions disappear. I’ve demanded answers from Governor Walz. I’ve pushed back when Minnesota Democrats tried to dodge accountability.
And now, the truth is coming out.
But here’s the reality: **the Democrats who allowed this to happen, and the Minnesota media that ignored it, are already working to protect themselves.** They don’t want reform. They want this story to fade.
That’s why I’m asking for your help today.
We need resources to keep exposing the fraud, defending the facts, and holding the political establishment accountable. The stakes couldn’t be higher. [_**If you’re able, please make a contribution today to help us fight back and finish this job.**_](https://Finsta11160a6bc.link.bmesend.com/api/LinkHandler/getaction2?%3d%3d)
---
| | [Donate Today](https://Finsta11160a6bc.link.bmesend.com/api/LinkHandler/getaction2?%3d%3d)
---
| Minnesotans deserve honesty, accountability, and leadership that refuses to look the other way. Thank you for standing with me. Brad Finstad

name: Michael Bennet
subject: something different from what I usually send
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
| | |
---
Friend – **I believe that, as Americans, we have a responsibility to build opportunity for one another and the next generation.**
It's been the guiding light of my career in public service – but it didn't come out of nowhere. It was instilled by the people who made me who I am today. So I'd like to fill you in on that story and how it's shaped my campaign.
But that means this is going to be a longer email. [So if you're short on time, before you click away, will you please chip in toward my $100,000 end-of-quarter goal ahead of our biggest fundraising deadline to date?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121873732/590860360/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1024-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTE2ZmNkNC01M2UwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j8gu58FYrnRi4Mlumcvr_Nmk4KMXtalv3TXCN3ynhHc=)
As some of you know, my mom was born in 1938 in Poland right before Hitler’s invasion. My mom and her parents were separated from each other for years during the war, but miraculously survived. After the war they went first to Sweden and to Mexico City and then to New York.
For my mom and her parents, America was a land of peace and freedom, a beacon of hope and opportunity and a chance to rebuild their shattered lives.
As her parents adjusted and started a business, Mom enrolled herself in New York City's public schools. She went on to be an elementary school librarian and married my dad – a dedicated public servant, like his dad before him. And eventually, they had me.
**My parents made sure that we knew how lucky we were, that we didn't let the opportunities before us go to waste, and that we had an obligation to make the world a better place.**
These are the same lessons my wife, Susan, and I strive to pass on to our daughters. They're what has motivated me to fight for reforms that make our economy work for everyone, not just the wealthiest few, every day throughout my years in the U.S. Senate.
But after watching Donald Trump spend all of 2025 taking a wrecking ball to our economy and the Constitution, and chipping away at democracy, it's clear he didn't learn the same things. **Now, my generation is the first one at risk of handing our kids less opportunity, not more.** It's an insult to the generations who came before us, like my parents and grandparents.
And with Trump's party holding the levers of power in the federal government, I'm not holding my breath for Washington to deliver the solutions to the problems we're facing. From lowering the costs of housing, health care, and child care to fighting government corruption to rebuilding the middle class, the solutions Colorado needs will have to start here in Colorado, by Coloradans.
**So now, I feel obligated to keep fighting to make the world a better place, where I know I can make the biggest difference: back in my home state.**
**_[That's why I'm running for governor – and if you agree that our kids and grandkids deserve a governor who will fight for their future, then I hope you'll make an investment in our success by chipping in $5, $25, or anything you can to help me close the gap ahead of my final quarterly deadline coming up in](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121873733/590860361/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1024-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTE2ZmNkNC01M2UwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j8gu58FYrnRi4Mlumcvr_Nmk4KMXtalv3TXCN3ynhHc=)eight days._**
| | [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121873734/590860362/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1024-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTE2ZmNkNC01M2UwLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j8gu58FYrnRi4Mlumcvr_Nmk4KMXtalv3TXCN3ynhHc=)
---
If you made it this far, thanks for reading – and for being a part of this grassroots team.

Michael

name: Luis Vidal
subject: Help save the giant sequoia
body:
https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/donate-rrf [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/donate-rrf]
Dear Peter,
“Give us a good burn.” Those were the words spoken by Chairman Gomez of the Tübatulabal Tribe as we gathered beneath the ancient sequoias at Alder Creek last year.
For the first time in more than a century, cultural fire returned to these ancestral lands in 2024, with multiple cultural burns at Alder Creek since then. Leaders of the Tule River Indian Tribe, North Fork Mono Tribe, and Tübatulabal Tribe lit small, intentional flames. Part ceremony, part restoration.
Each spark honored generations of ecological knowledge and a shared purpose: restoring balance to a forest that evolved to thrive with fire.
Help restore balance to the forest: Make your matched gift to the Redwood Resilience Fund [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/donate-rrf]
When you give to the Redwood Resilience Fund, you’re helping strengthen the bond between cultural wisdom and conservation science to protect forests that have sheltered life for millennia.The Redwood Resilience Fund is a gift to the future [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/donate-rrf]
At Alder Creek Grove, home to hundreds of ancient sequoias—including the Stagg Tree, the fifth-largest tree on Earth—cultural burns are helping to protect a living legacy. These low-intensity fires help to clear overgrowth and nurture young sequoias, ensuring these giants endure in an era of climate change. It’s a practice that restores both forest and culture.
The banning of these burning practices was a significant factor in losing nearly 20% of the world’s oldest giant sequoias in the high-severity megafires of the last decade. Let’s work to make sure that never happens again. [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/donate-rrf]
Through the Redwood Resilience Fund, Save the Redwoods League is deepening these partnerships, supporting ongoing stewardship at Alder Creek, and safeguarding habitat for species such as the Pacific fisher and the California spotted owl. Will you join us in this urgent work?
Make your matched gift to the Redwood Resilience Fund by midnight 12/31 and help keep the sequoias’ story of renewal burning bright. [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/donate-rrf]
Photo of Luis Vidal [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/STRWL/STRWL/1/89574/images/Mask%20group.png] With gratitude,
Luis Vidal
Giant Sequoia Stewardship Manager
Save the Redwoods League
Help restore and protect the redwoods

Help restore balance to the forest: Make your matched gift to the Redwood Resilience Fund [] When you give to the Redwood Resilience Fund, you’re helping strengthen the bond between cultural wisdom and conservation science to protect forests that have sheltered life for millennia.The Redwood Resilience Fund is a gift to the future [] At Alder Creek Grove, home to hundreds of ancient sequoias—including the Stagg Tree, the fifth-largest tree on Earth—cultural burns are helping to protect a living legacy. These low-intensity fires help to clear overgrowth and nurture young sequoias, ensuring these giants endure in an era of climate change. It’s a practice that restores both forest and culture. The banning of these burning practices was a significant factor in losing nearly 20% of the world’s oldest giant sequoias in the high-severity megafires of the last decade. Let’s work to make sure that never happens again. [] Through the Redwood Resilience Fund, Save the Redwoods League is deepening these partnerships, supporting ongoing stewardship at Alder Creek, and safeguarding habitat for species such as the Pacific fisher and the California spotted owl. Will you join us in this urgent work? Make your matched gift to the Redwood Resilience Fund by midnight 12/31 and help keep the sequoias’ story of renewal burning bright. [] Photo of Luis Vidal [] With gratitude, Luis Vidal Giant Sequoia Stewardship Manager Save the Redwoods League Help restore and protect the redwoods DONATE NOW [] [] Save the Redwoods League 111 Sutter Street, 11th Floor San Francisco, CA 94104 United States CONNECT WITH US [] [] [] [] [] [] Manage Preferences [] | Unsubscribe []

# [Amped Up w/ Cliff Schecter & David Shuster; And Guest, Former MSNBC Host Katie Phang!](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182375285&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMzc1Mjg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY1MzE1MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2OTEyMzUxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.050pK1JVX00ba_Yx_d5HBQDvncwmAm-9kHDI-ijUnJk)
### Cliff Schecter and David Shuster are joined by legal analyst Katie Phang to break down the latest on Epstein, the DOJ, due process, and accountability
| | [Cliff Schecter](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge), [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/@blueamp), [David Shuster](https://substack.com/@davidshuster), and [Katie Phang](https://substack.com/@katiephang)
---
| Dec 23
---
| [](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)| | [](https://substack.com/@davidshuster)| | [](https://substack.com/@katiephang)
---|---|---|---|---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182375285&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMzc1Mjg1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NjUzMTUxNywiZXhwIjoxNzY5MTIzNTE3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzcyMjUzMiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9gjJPxTDO-H1m02Ot3blmQMy6ScesbfCYbV1oTkCihU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182375285&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMzc1Mjg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY1MzE1MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2OTEyMzUxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.050pK1JVX00ba_Yx_d5HBQDvncwmAm-9kHDI-ijUnJk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182375285&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMzc1Mjg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY1MzE1MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2OTEyMzUxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.050pK1JVX00ba_Yx_d5HBQDvncwmAm-9kHDI-ijUnJk)
---
| | [](https://substack..9rcK4XCJckk0EjNqAEx3Eoi2IKLIUZuYRx4HGfg3d94?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/blueamp/p/amped-up-w-cliff-schecter-and-david?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c8e1bdc1-48f5-4110-861d-d0191e771464?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This was one of those special shows I LOVE doing. You know the ones, where the chemistry just clicks like JD Vance's heels when he goose steps. I had a blast digging into the news with David Shuster, per usual, and the brilliant, funny, charismatic trial-lawyer-turned-MSNBC-host-turned-independent-media sensation, Katie Phang.
Katie, unsurprisingly, was on her game from the jump.
Our conversation moved fast, covered a lot of ground, and it sure as hell never lost its edge--exactly what happens when you put legal expertise, political savvy and truth-telling--i.e. some folks who aren't afraid to call bullshit when they see it--together.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4a9619ba-e640-4736-ab0e-175f6db956ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
We spent the bulk of _Amped Up_ digging into the latest releases connected to the Epstein files, not chasing conspiracy--which the Trump Administration may have killed as it's seemingly brought most of them to life--but focusing on accountability, transparency, and a disturbing pattern of institutional failure that surrounds them.
Katie brought precision, legal clarity, and a refusal to let process be used as a shield against responsibility. She was careful, factual, and unflinching about what the law requires--and what the Department of Justice is currently failing to deliver. Yet she was able to fit this in between humor, even cursing more than me (!), and just a really great conversation in general.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4a9619ba-e640-4736-ab0e-175f6db956ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
What caught my attention in what Katie shared wasn't just what's in the documents, _but how they 've been released_. Rolling disclosures. Sloppy redactions. No clear explanations.
In other words, [par for the course with the clown car that just won't empty](https://substack.com/redirect/dcc86851-9a13-4534-8a76-e5548eb0b1c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This group of no-nothing numskulls are every bit as amateur when they try to hide their alleged crimes as they are when they commit them.
Katie broke down why that matters legally, not just politically, which was key to our show, as David and I are most certainly not lawyers. When agencies ignore statutory timelines or redact information without justification, it isn't just a case of bad optics--it undermines public trust and re-traumatizes survivors who were promised transparency.
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.twThpDQfk5PiU7Rl_xQVyqvZZJpkanZzPDBuEJx9Pts?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=182375285)
* * *
David added crucial perspective from his years as an anchor inside cable media as it was transforming from mainstream media into the current corporate monstrosity, and I added perspective from my years as an on-air analyst on a number of stations.
We talked honestly about how stories get softened, delayed, or quietly sidelined--_and[why independent media is often where the real accountability work happens now](https://substack.com/redirect/4a9619ba-e640-4736-ab0e-175f6db956ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. That wasn't a knock on journalism as a craft. It was a sober assessment of how institutional pressure shapes what the public ultimately sees.
We also zoomed out. This wasn't just about Epstein. It was about due process, separation of powers, and the dangerous normalization of executive overreach. Katie was very clear that defending due process does not mean defending criminals, as Republican mouth-breathing partisans like to growl.
**It means defending the Constitution.[When that principle gets twisted into a partisan talking point, we're already in deep trouble](https://substack.com/redirect/89eb3360-58e9-40bd-818c-92f13561e5c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**
* * *
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182375285&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMzc1Mjg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY1MzE1MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2OTEyMzUxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.050pK1JVX00ba_Yx_d5HBQDvncwmAm-9kHDI-ijUnJk)
* * *
Toward the end of the show, we talked about media itself. After briefly going into the tragedy of 60 Minutes, with Bari Weiss, the new "editor" at CBS News making it Trump TV. One of her first acts? Suppressing a segment on how without due process we sent innocent men to a torture prison (CECOT) in El Salvador. [It is horrific, and it was predicted](https://substack.com/redirect/89eb3360-58e9-40bd-818c-92f13561e5c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Katie then talked more about the media in general, including why she left MSNBC, what she misses, and what she's gained in her life by building something independent where she can live her values. She was honest about the tradeoff of leaving security behind to speak more freely.
Luckily for us, she made the choice she did, because her authentic voice right now is exactly what the moment demands.
This was a serious conversation grounded in the simple concept of right vs wrong. Not left vs right. Accountability vs impunity. Transparency vs obfuscation. And damn it, we made some jokes and had a helluva time while doing it. That's what I call time well spent.
And that's why this episode matters--and why I'm grateful to Katie Phang for bringing her expertise, her integrity, and her clarity to _Amped Up_ this holiday season.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e21a9e36-ad1a-44c9-aa18-6cef0d579fbd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| A guest post by| [Katie Phang](https://substack.com/@katiephang?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)Truth-Teller | Independent Journalist and Legal Analyst | Trial Lawyer | Fighting for OUR Democracy | "Law and Disorder" Substack | "Though She Be But Little, She Is Fierce"[Subscribe to Katie](https://substack.com/redirect/98b05fa4-6df3-4f91-9c6f-90cc91e5bddb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

---
| | [Comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182375285&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMzc1Mjg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY1MzE1MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2OTEyMzUxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.050pK1JVX00ba_Yx_d5HBQDvncwmAm-9kHDI-ijUnJk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

name: Adam Schiff
subject: Why I’m standing with Chris.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
The most important fundraising deadline so far is almost here — Chris needs your help to win in 2026.
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251223_2.v0CAec&refcode=em20251223-2&t=1&akid=4115%2E582294%2EJpYupK)
Hi. This is Senator Adam Schiff.
I’m reaching out to tell you why I’m supporting my friend Chris Pappas in the race for New Hampshire’s Senate seat. He’s exactly the type of leader we need right now in the Senate — and exactly the type of fighter we need right now to keep this seat blue.
[**The biggest fundraising deadline so far is almost here, and we need to show our support for Chris. Will you chip in $5 now?**](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251223_2.v0CAec&refcode=em20251223-2&t=2&akid=4115%2E582294%2EJpYupK)
**There is no path to winning the Senate without New Hampshire. Full stop.**
Republicans know that, and it’s why they’re doing everything they can to recruit high-profile candidates to defeat Chris.
They’ve already convinced two former Senators to run against Chris: **Scott Brown and John Sununu.**
The good news? New Hampshire knows Chris. They know he’s not about talk, he’s about getting things done. They know he’s one of the hardest workers in Congress, fighting to lower costs, take on Big Pharma, and stand up for veterans.
That’s why he’s won in one of the most competitive districts in the country four times in a row — and it’s exactly why he’s going to win this Senate seat — with your help.
But we can’t take anything for granted. Republicans have their sights set on New Hampshire. And this upcoming end-of-year fundraising report is a big deal.
**This next week is setting the stage for November 2026.** What Chris raises between now and the end of the month determines the strength of this campaign and whether Chris can continue delivering for New Hampshire.
[**Can you please chip in $5 or anything you can right now to help make the strongest end-of-year fundraising report possible for Chris?**](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251223_2.v0CAec&refcode=em20251223-2&t=3&akid=4115%2E582294%2EJpYupK)
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251223_2.oVmYO4&amount=5&refcode=em20251223-2&t=22&akid=4115%2E582294%2EJpYupK)**
I look forward to working alongside Chris in the U.S. Senate to deliver for all Americans.
Thank you,
Adam Schiff
U.S. Senator

clean_body: The most important fundraising deadline so far is almost here — Chris needs your help to win in 2026. Hi. This is Senator Adam Schiff. I’m reaching out to tell you why I’m supporting my friend Chris Pappas in the race for New Hampshire’s Senate seat. He’s exactly the type of leader we need right now in the Senate — and exactly the type of fighter we need right now to keep this seat blue. There is no path to winning the Senate without New Hampshire. Full stop. Republicans know that, and it’s why they’re doing everything they can to recruit high-profile candidates to defeat Chris. They’ve already convinced two former Senators to run against Chris: Scott Brown and John Sununu. The good news? New Hampshire knows Chris. They know he’s not about talk, he’s about getting things done. They know he’s one of the hardest workers in Congress, fighting to lower costs, take on Big Pharma, and stand up for veterans. That’s why he’s won in one of the most competitive districts in the country four times in a row — and it’s exactly why he’s going to win this Senate seat — with your help. But we can’t take anything for granted. Republicans have their sights set on New Hampshire. And this upcoming end-of-year fundraising report is a big deal. This next week is setting the stage for November 2026. What Chris raises between now and the end of the month determines the strength of this campaign and whether Chris can continue delivering for New Hampshire. I look forward to working alongside Chris in the U.S. Senate to deliver for all Americans. Thank you, Adam Schiff U.S. Senator New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: I miss you, Peter...
body:
I’d really appreciate it if you opened this email

| | | | Alright, Peter, I’m just going to cut to the chase and say it: I MISS YOU!
For years, you’ve stood by me: stepping in when I needed it most and even generously contributing your hard-earned money to advance our conservative agenda.
**_And now, Peter, I’m asking you to stand with me once more... because this fight is far from over!_****_
_**Together, we were victorious in 2024, but as we head into the midterms, we will need to work twice as hard to maintain our Republican majority and continue providing President Trump with the fuel he needs to Make America Great Again.
Can I count on you again, Peter?
I swear to you, this isn’t a routine update; [it’s the most important thing you’ll read today:](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIyM19GSS1XNS4xMTAxMTdfdDE3ODE2NjAtMTA0MyZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMjNfRkktVzUuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzgxNjYwLTEwNDMifQ/6OLAZWk6XobIIVKN0BBzRw) | [ READ HERE >> ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIyM19GSS1XNS4xMTAxMTdfdDE3ODE2NjAtMTA0MyZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMjNfRkktVzUuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzgxNjYwLTEwNDMifQ/OeSRwzu9apU43oAvZ5MAZg)

Peter,
We build our movement by affirming that we are stronger together.
It’s how we create the Texas of our dreams, a state where EVERY person – no matter the color of their skin, the nation they’re from, or the language they speak – can live with freedom and dignity.
With the holiday season upon us and a big midterm year ahead of us, it’s crucial we end 2025 on a high fundraising note. Will you help us make that happen, Peter?
PITCH IN to TOP PAC to give us the boost we need to start 2026 off strong! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=ClosingOutStrong&amount=15.00&refcode2=0a8e40fb-55e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa]

It will take ALL of us bringing our best selves to win the future Texans deserve – a future where our rights are respected, our families are prospering, and our communities are thriving.
Manifest this vision with us by DONATING TODAY. It’s the end of the year, so every contribution – no matter the size – goes a long way.

I am a West Point graduate and former Apache helicopter pilot. I have flown combat missions overseas and faced real danger, but nothing threatens Michigan’s future like the political elites running our state into the ground. **I have never been afraid of terrorists or enemy fire, and I am certainly not afraid of Gretchen Whitmer and the Radical Left.** Just like in the Army, victory depends on the strength of the team beside me. name: =?UTF-8?Q?Eduardo_Ver=C3=A1stegui?=
subject: This Christmas… will you help bring a child home?
body:
A child should fall asleep on Christmas Eve listening for reindeer hooves...

| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e33461483/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvOUFaTXc1VkM0UA/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsdlI0U2VtTA/qP2hpZHM9bmNrTkJTdkV5SDE3NWxscw/gaUslRQ/s6u1c81525c)
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Friend, A child should fall asleep on Christmas Eve listening for reindeer hooves... **Not to footsteps in the hallway outside a locked room.** **Right now through December 31, your gift to the Sound of Freedom Foundation will be MATCHED—dollar for dollar—so you can do DOUBLE the good for God’s children.**
---
* **$25 = $50**
* **$50 = $100**
* **$100 = $200**

---
But this opportunity ends **at midnight on December 31.**
---
[ **Double My Gift Before December 31** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
My name is **Eduardo Verástegui**. I produced the film _Sound of Freedom_ , and I now serve as President of the **Sound of Freedom Foundation**. I want you to know: **I've given my life to fight for God’s children who are trapped in the nightmare of sex trafficking.** Right now, while families are hanging lights and wrapping presents, there are children who don’t even know what day it is. They are far from home. No tree. No lights. No family. Only the constant terror of being used, bought, and sold by adults who see them as a product — not as sons and daughters. Some parents are staring at an empty seat at the table this Christmas.
A stocking that will stay limp.
A bed that’s made... and cold. That is the brutal reality of child sex trafficking. And this Christmas, I’m asking you to do something beautiful and bold: **help bring a child home for Christmas.**__
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
If you watched _Sound of Freedom_ and thought, “I wish there were something real I could do,” this is that moment. Because a movie is not enough. We needed a mission.
---
We launched the Sound of Freedom Foundation to:
* partner with trusted investigators, law enforcement, and aftercare teams to help **locate and rescue children

**
* help build the evidence needed to **arrest and prosecute traffickers

**
* support survivors with **safe shelter and trauma-informed care** so they can begin to heal.

---
All of this flows from one unshakable conviction... **God’s children are NOT for sale.** And here is something I need you to understand: we cannot do any of this without people like you. Every investigation we help fuel...
every safe bed prepared for the moment a child walks free...
every trauma counselor who sits with a survivor as the nightmares spill out...
every step toward healing and restoration... All of it depends on someone deciding: **“Not on my watch. Not this Christmas.”** That’s you. When you give, you step into the story as the hero. We are simply the guide—connecting your courage and generosity to children who need it most. And right now, your compassion goes twice as far. [**Give before December 31 and your gift is matched dollar for dollar.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ Double My Gift: Bring a Child Home ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
I want you to picture a moment with me. A child has been rescued. For the first time in a long time, they sleep in a safe bed. They wake up to soft light instead of harsh neon.
Someone tells them, **“You’re safe now. No one here will hurt you.”** Behind the scenes, a team is working—confirming identities, contacting authorities, and searching for family members who have been praying, weeping, and waiting. Sometimes it takes months.
Sometimes years. Then one day, in an airport or at a border crossing, a door opens... a mother sees the child she thought was gone forever... there are no speeches, no cameras—just tears, shaking shoulders, and a whispered, **“Thank You, God.”** **This is the kind of miracle your generosity can help make possible.** Not every reunion happens exactly on December 25th. But the heart of Christmas—**God stepping into our darkness and bringing His children home—is what drives everything we do.** Traffickers don’t take holidays off.
So we can’t either. That’s why I’m asking you—urgently and personally—to make your year-end gift **before midnight on December 31** , while it will still be doubled.
---
[ Yes, Double My Gift Today ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Because somewhere, right now, there is a child who feels utterly forgotten. Your gift is a message that whispers into that darkness: **“I see you. I’m fighting for you. I’m helping bring you home.”** **[Will you help bring a child home for Christmas?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)**
---
With gratitude, Eduardo Verástegui
Sound of Freedom, Producer
For the Sound of Freedom Foundation
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
P.S. The match ends**December 31 at midnight. Please give now so your gift will be doubled.**
---
[ Support the Sound of Freedom Foundation ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
6671 Southwest Freeway, Suite 700, Houston TX 77074
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e33461483/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjM/LbmNsdlI0U2VtTA/qP2hpZHM9bmNrTkJTdkV5SDE3NWxscw/gaUslRQ/s3t02f0a7b8)

* * *
### The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication
### Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism
[Support What We Do](https://substack.com/redirect/e5ce63fe-e364-4c62-add9-215816c935c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
**Yes, They Know It 's Christmas**
_By Michael Matheson Miller_
The 1984 song _Do They Know It 's Christmas?_ was written to turn the world's attention to famine in Ethiopia. It succeeded at raising awareness and millions of dollars those in need. But for 40 years, the song's inevitable return every holiday season has perpetuated a false picture of Africans.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4d011f12-832a-4036-adce-a59af5b86689?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Despite good intentions, the song is clueless. For instance, Ethiopia is home to some of the most ancient Christian communities. In fact, one of the first Christian converts mentioned in the New Testament is an Ethiopian baptized by Philip. Yet Bob Geldof, Bono, George Michael, Boy George, Sting, and the rest of the Band Aid "supergroup" ask with straight faces: "Do they know it's Christmas time at all?"
[](https://substack.com/redirect/cfcbb260-f25a-43bf-b505-9dc0aefb2656?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The answer is yes. They knew then and they know now. But there is a bigger problem. As Senegalese entrepreneur Magatte Wade says in our film, _Poverty, Inc.,_ _Do They Know It 's Christmas_ portrays a sentimental vision of Africans as helpless and dependent on Western largesse. Who are the protagonists of development in Africa? According to Band Aid, it's up to you, generous Westerner, to "feed the world."
It sounds curmudgeonly to critique a pop song, but unfortunately the paternalism expressed in _Do They Know It 's Christmas?_ underlies much of the West's attitude and concrete policies of managing the developing world. Anyone who critiques the foreign aid model as ineffective or harmful is liable to accusations of being "heart dead and brain dead," as Bono of U2 once put it.
According to defenders of the status quo, without foreign aid millions of people would die. This claim is made despite decades of evidence showing there is little to no correlation between foreign aid and economic development. It also makes no distinction between emergency aid and the thousands of projects and programs managed by USAID.
The recent DOGE - USAID shake-ups have focused on corruption and waste as the impetus for rethinking US policy. Putting a spotlight on the absurd pet projects of unaccountable bureaucrats--with no obvious connection to actually helping poor people--was critical to exposing the many failures of USAID.
But keeping the focus on corruption and waste in reforms of US foreign assistance risks missing the bigger picture. The fundamental problem of global aid is not fraud. It's exclusion. I'll highlight two examples.
First, the foreign aid model spends an enormous amount of time and money on health care, education, and infrastructure. No doubt these are important aspects of a flourishing society.
But ask yourself two questions. One, isn't the purpose of development to help countries become independent? Yet we've been doing the same things since the end of World War II. Second, if you have good health, education, and roads, but you don't have private property, access to justice in the courts, or the ability to start a business and participate in the formal economy, what would you do? The rational response is to emigrate, join the political or criminal classes, or despair.
As the late Ghanaian entrepreneur Herman Chinery-Hesse explains in Poverty, Inc. "You are stuck in a hole with all your skills and talents, and that is unfortunately the way it is."
By focusing our foreign assistance on infrastructure projects, our aid often has the unintended effect of propping up corrupt regimes. As Michael Fairbanks explains, it cuts the link between the government and its people. Foreign aid subsidizes bad government and creates incentives for governments _not to build_ the institutions of justice that would enable people to create wealth. If a government has no incentives to build a commercial society it won't.
A second key problem is that foreign aid typically follows a "disaster relief" model. No serious critic of foreign aid argues against extending assistance during an emergency or natural disaster. We in the wealthy West have a moral obligation to help. But we've used the emergency response approach to solve chronic problems. It has not worked.
People are not poor because they lack material goods. They are poor because they are excluded from the institutions of justice that would enable them to create prosperity in their own families and communities. These are things that we take for granted in the United States, but are missing throughout Africa and the developing world -things like property rights and clear title to land, access to justice in the courts, and the ability to register your business and participate in the formal economy. This especially impacts women, minorities, and the poorest who lack political and social connections.
What do you think the United States would be like if we didn't have property rights and rule of law? Do you think entrepreneurs would take risks and build companies if they could be confiscated without cause?
The United States is not perfect, but it is the expert in creating entrepreneurial societies with widespread opportunity and social mobility that attracts millions of immigrants every year. But instead of actually helping countries build the institutions that enable prosperity, USAID promoted on disproven theories and ephemeral and fashionable causes that perpetuated the global poverty industry.
The United States should not cease doing development. But it should avoid paternalistic, sentimental, and neo-colonialist policies that delay the development of business, and harm the US image. It should stop subsidizing corrupt leaders abroad and crony capitalists and entrenched bureaucrats at home. Instead, it should partner with countries to help build rule of law and create the conditions for enterprise so that people can create prosperity in their own communities and become independent engines of enterprise and growth.
Poor people are not objects of our charity, our pity, or our compassion. They are subjects and should be the protagonists of their own story of development. There will always be a need for generosity, help, and charity, but the point is not just to "do something." As we say in Poverty, Inc, while we need a heart for the poor. We also need a mind for the poor.
It is time to re-think poverty. The fundamental question is not how to alleviate poverty, but how to help create the conditions for people to create wealth. The restructuring of USAID has been messy and volatile, but it is an opportunity to build a different relationship with Africa and the developing world. Rather than listening to the voices of pop stars or the leaders of the poverty industry, it's a chance to listen to a different chorus of voices, people like Magatte Wade and entrepreneurs and innovators who are charting a better way forward.
_**You can view my recent conversation with Magatte Wade,[here](https://substack.com/redirect/1ffe8d7d-177d-4452-bce1-70b613a213b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**_
* * *
* * *
_**Michael Matheson Miller is a Senior Research Fellow and Chief of Strategic Initiatives at the Acton Institute. He is the director of the Center for Social Flourishing and is the director and producer of the documentary, Poverty, Inc.**_
